t.es: Toca una bebida para editarla. Mantén pulsada y arrastra la bebida para cambiar su orden.
t.fr: Appuie sur une boisson pour la modifier. Maintiens appuyé, puis fais glisser pour réorganiser.
t.hi: किसी पेय को बदलने के लिए उस पर टैप करो. उसका क्रम बदलने के लिए पेय को पकड़ो और खींचो.
t.id: Ketuk minuman untuk mengeditnya. Tahan dan seret minuman untuk mengubah letaknya.
t.it: Tocca una bevanda per modificarla. Tieni premuto e trascina per riordinarle.
t.ja: ドリンクをタップすると編集できます。長押ししてドラッグすると並び替えられます。
t.ko: 편집할 음료를 누르세요. 순서를 바꾸려면 길게 눌러 드래그하세요.
t.ms: Ketik minuman untuk mengeditnya. Pegang dan seret minuman untuk mengubah susunannya.
t.nb: Trykk på en drikk for å redigere den. Hold og dra drikken for å endre rekkefølgen.
t.nl: Tik op een drankje om het te bewerken. Houd het drankje vast en sleep het om de volgorde te wijzigen.
t.pl: Kliknij napój, aby go edytować. Przytrzymaj i przeciągnij napój, aby zmienić jego kolejność.
t.pt_BR: Toque em uma bebida para editar. Toque, segure e arraste para mudar a ordem.
t.ru: Нажми на напиток, чтобы отредактировать его. Зажми и перетащи, чтобы изменить порядок.
t.sv: Tryck på en dryck för att redigera den. Håll in och dra drycken för att ändra ordningen.
t.tr: Bir içeceği düzenlemek için üzerine dokun. Sırasını değiştirmek için içeceğe basılı tutup sürükle.
t.vi: Nhấp vào đồ uống để chỉnh sửa. Giữ và kéo đồ uống để thay đổi cách sắp xếp.
t.zh_CN: 点击饮料进行编辑。按住并拖动饮料以更改其排列位置。

context:
Surface: Drink parameters / choose beverage / create drink screen — entry-point row, "add new" cell, and navigation title for the creation flow.
Type: button label / row title / screen title
Context: Imperative verb. Opens the flow for creating a new custom beverage.

Surface: Drink parameters screen — section header above the list of beverages the user has hidden from the main catalog.
Type: section header
Context: Groups beverages that were hidden via the "Hide drink" action and can be restored.

Surface: Create / edit beverage screen — drink-name text field placeholder and accessibility label.
Type: placeholder
Context: Hint shown inside an empty text field where the user types the name of their custom beverage.
Constraints: Keep short (must fit a single-line text field on narrow screens).

Surface: Create / edit beverage screen — section header above the icon picker; also shown as a card label in onboarding "own drinks" promo.
Type: section header
Context: Label for the section where the user chooses an icon image for their custom beverage. Displayed uppercased on the create-drink screen.
Constraints: Keep short (single word preferred).

Surface: Create / edit beverage screen — section header above the color picker (displayed uppercased). Also used as the VoiceOver accessibility label of individual color swatch cells (DrinkColorCell) and as a card label in the onboarding "own drinks" paywall promo.
Type: section header / accessibility label
Context: Noun. Identifies the color attribute the user is choosing for their custom beverage. Displayed uppercased on the create-drink screen.
Constraints: Keep short (single word preferred).

Surface: Drink parameters screen — main screen title and accessibility label for the info cell describing beverage settings.
Type: screen title / row title
Context: Heading for the screen that lists beverage settings (icon, color, coefficient, visibility).

Surface: Create / edit beverage screen — slider title for the hydration coefficient; drink-parameters screen accessibility label; explainer popup title.
Type: section header / setting label
Context: Label for the hydration coefficient of a beverage — how much a given drink counts towards the daily water goal (e.g. 100% for water, ~50% for coffee). Refers to the body's hydration level, not the app's "water balance" finance metaphor.
Constraints: Keep short (single line on narrow screens).

en: New Year discount [%s], start drinking more water with us!
context:
Surface: Main screen seasonal discount offer cell — title of the New Year promo card.
Type: section header / promo card title
Context: Promotes the New Year subscription discount from the main screen offer cell. The first line shows the discount percentage; the second line is a seasonal hook inviting the user to start drinking more water with the app. Sibling of newYearDiscount (compact title without the second line).
Placeholders: [%s] — discount percentage including the percent sign (e.g. "50%").
Tone: promotional, seasonal, encouraging.

t.ar: خصم رأس السنة [%s]، ابدأ بشرب المزيد من الماء معنا!
t.da: Nytårsrabat [%s], begynd at drikke mere vand med os!
t.de: Neujahrsrabatt [%s], fang an, mit uns mehr Wasser zu trinken!
t.en: New Year discount [%s], start drinking more water with us!
t.es: Descuento del [%s] por Año Nuevo, ¡empieza a beber más agua con nosotros!
t.fr: Réduction du Nouvel An [%s], bois plus d'eau avec nous !
t.hi: नए साल का डिस्काउंट [%s], हमारे साथ ज़्यादा पानी पीना शुरू करो!
t.id: Diskon Tahun Baru [%s], mulai minum lebih banyak air dengan kami!
t.it: Sconto di Capodanno [%s], inizia a bere più acqua con noi!
t.ja: [%s]オフの年末年始割引、一緒にもっと水を飲みましょう！
t.ko: 새해 할인 [%s], 우리와 함께 더 많은 물을 마시기 시작하세요!
t.ms: Diskaun Tahun Baharu [%s], mulakan minum lebih banyak air dengan kami!
t.nb: Nyttårsrabatt [%s], begynn å drikke mer vann med oss!
t.nl: [%s] nieuwjaarskorting, ga met ons meer water drinken!
t.pl: Zniżka noworoczna [%s], zacznij pić z nami więcej wody!
t.pt_BR: Desconto de Ano Novo [%s], comece a beber mais água conosco!
t.ru: Новогодняя скидка [%s], начни пить больше воды вместе с нами!
t.sv: Nyårsrabatt [%s], börja dricka mer vatten med oss!
t.tr: [%s] Yeni Yıl indirimi, bizimle daha fazla su iç!
t.vi: Ưu đãi năm mới [%s], bắt đầu uống thêm nước với chúng tôi!
t.zh_CN: 新年优惠 [%s]，开始和我们一起多多饮水！

context:
Surface: New Year discount paywall / promo popup (DiscountOfferFullViewController, DiscountOfferBlueCardView) — countdown timer caption above the remaining-time value.
Type: label / caption
Context: Short caption introducing the live countdown of the seasonal New Year discount offer. The remaining time value is rendered separately next to / below this label.
Constraints: Keep short — single line on narrow screens; trailing colon is part of the label.
Tone: promotional, urgent.

en: Drink water and be healthy in the New Year with our app
context:
Surface: New Year seasonal promo paywall — motivational paragraph (currently no live Swift call-sites; preserved for legacy / re-introduced New Year promo layouts).
Type: motivational text
Context: Seasonal New Year framing that ties drinking water (with help of the app) to being healthy in the new year. Shown as supporting copy on the New Year discount paywall.
Register: casual T-form — main-screen motivational text is casual companion voice (default for non-formal surfaces).
Tone: promotional, seasonal, encouraging.

t.ar: اشرب الماء وكن في صحة جيدة في رأس السنة مع تطبيقنا
t.da: Drik vand og vær sund i det nye år med vores app
t.de: Trinke Wasser und komme gesund durchs neue Jahr mit unserer App
t.en: Drink water and be healthy in the New Year with our app
t.es: Bebe agua y cuida tu salud en Año Nuevo con nuestra aplicación
t.fr: Bois de l'eau et commence une nouvelle année en bonne santé avec notre appli
t.hi: हमारे ऐप के साथ नए साल में पानी पियो और स्वस्थ रहो
t.id: Minum air dan jadi sehat di Tahun Baru dengan aplikasi kami
t.it: Bevi acqua e stai in salute nel nuovo anno con la nostra app
t.ja: 新年は、アプリと一緒に水を飲んで健康になりましょう
t.ko: 새해에도 우리 앱과 함께 물을 마시고 건강하세요
t.ms: Minum air dan sihat pada Tahun Baharu dengan aplikasi kami
t.nb: Drikk vann og vær sunn i det nye året med appen vår
t.nl: Drink water en wees gezond in het nieuwe jaar met onze app
t.pl: Pij wodę i dbaj o zdrowie w Nowym Roku z pomocą naszej aplikacji
t.pt_BR: Beba água e seja saudável no Ano Novo com nosso aplicativo
t.ru: Пей воду и будь здоров в Новом году с нашим приложением
t.sv: Drick vatten och håll dig frisk under det nya året med vår app
t.tr: Yeni Yılda uygulamamız ile su iç ve sağlıklı ol
t.vi: Uống nước và giữ sức khỏe trong năm mới với ứng dụng này
t.zh_CN: 用我们的应用多喝水，在新的一年保持健康

context:
Surface: New Year discount paywall — old / pre-discount price row on the subscription button (DiscountPriceButton).
Type: label
Context: Prefix shown next to the original (non-discounted) subscription price on the New Year discount paywall, e.g. "Old price $39.99". The formatted price is concatenated with a space at runtime.
Constraints: Keep short — must fit on one line next to the price; no trailing punctuation.
Tone: promotional.

Surface: New Year discount paywall — discounted / special price row on the subscription button (DiscountPriceButton).
Type: label
Context: Prefix shown next to the discounted New Year subscription price, e.g. "Special price $19.99". The formatted price is concatenated with a space at runtime. Note: legacy key name has a typo ("SpecialPrie" — missing "c") — do not rename; the user-facing value is correct.
Constraints: Keep short — must fit on one line next to the price; no trailing punctuation.
Tone: promotional.

Surface: Main screen seasonal discount offer cell — compact title variant of the New Year promo card (DiscountOfferCellViewModel).
Type: section header / promo card title
Context: Short version of the New Year discount headline used when the layout shows the discount percentage without the secondary "start drink more water with us" line. Sibling of newYearSaleTitle (two-line variant).
Placeholders: [%s] — discount percentage including the percent sign (e.g. "50%"); empty string is passed while the discount value is still loading.
Tone: promotional, seasonal.

en: Start the New Year with a healthy habit. We will help!
context:
Surface: Main screen seasonal discount offer cell — subtitle paragraph under the New Year promo title (DiscountOfferCellViewModel).
Type: paragraph (promo subtitle)
Context: Supporting line under the New Year discount headline, framing the offer as a chance to "start the New Year with a healthy habit" with help from the app.
Tone: promotional, seasonal, encouraging.

t.ar: ابدأ العام الجديد بعادة صحية. سنساعدك!
t.da: Start det nye år med en sund vane. Vi vil hjælpe dig!
t.de: Beginne das neue Jahr mit einer gesunden Angewohnheit. Wir helfen dabei!
t.en: Start the New Year with a healthy habit. We will help!
t.es: Empieza el año nuevo con un hábito saludable. ¡Te ayudaremos!
t.fr: Commence la nouvelle année avec une habitude saine. On t'aidera !
t.hi: नए साल की शुरुआत स्वस्थ आदत से करो। हम मदद करेंगे!
t.id: Mulai Tahun Baru dengan kebiasaan sehat. Kami bantu!
t.it: Inizia il Nuovo Anno con sane abitudini. Noi ti aiuteremo!
t.ja: 健康的な習慣で新年を始めましょう。お手伝いします！
t.ko: 건강한 습관으로 새해를 시작하세요. My Water가 도와줄게요!
t.ms: Mulakan Tahun Baharu dengan tabiat yang sihat. Kami akan membantu!
t.nb: Start det nye året med en sunn vane. Vi hjelper deg!
t.nl: Begin het nieuwe jaar met een gezonde gewoonte. Wij helpen je!
t.pl: Rozpocznij Nowy Rok zdrowym nawykiem. Pomożemy!
t.pt_BR: Comece o Ano Novo com hábitos saudáveis. Nós vamos ajudar!
t.ru: Начни Новый год со здоровой привычки. Мы поможем!
t.sv: Börja det nya året med en hälsosam vana. Vi hjälper till!
t.tr: Yeni Yıla sağlıklı bir alışkanlıkla başla. Yardımcı olacağız!
t.vi: Bắt đầu năm mới với thói quen tốt. Chúng tôi sẽ giúp bạn!
t.zh_CN: 以健康的习惯开启新的一年。我们会帮你的！

context:
Surface: Settings screen — entry row title that opens the year-in-review (annual recap) digest.
Type: settings row title
Context: Label on the settings row that lets the user re-open their year-in-review recap. Direct second-person address ("your"). Note: "didgest" key spelling is a legacy typo of "digest" — preserve the key, only translate the value.
Placeholders:
  [%i] — calendar year (4-digit integer, e.g. 2023)
Register: casual T-form — this key was corrected to casual T-form in the pre-G6 audit sweep (2026-05-16; see `loc_audit_prompt.md` — Binding operator decisions); do NOT regress to V-form on translation.
Tone: celebratory (year-in-review summary).

Surface: Main screen — section header on the year-in-review (annual recap) digest entry card; also used as the screen title inside the digest itself.
Type: section header / screen title
Context: Short title naming the year-in-review feature. Despite the legacy key name "didgestFor2021", the string is reused across years — the source value does not embed a specific year. Note: "didgest" key spelling is a legacy typo of "digest" — preserve the key, only translate the value.
Constraints: Keep short (≤30 chars) — fits as a section header / screen title.
Tone: celebratory (year-in-review summary).

Surface: app-wide — Main screen "Hide" CTA on the alcohol-warning section (MainViewModel); "Rate the app" cell close-button accessibility label + title (RateTheAppCell).
Type: button label
Context: Imperative verb. Generic "hide / dismiss this card" action — removes a cell or banner from view without permanently deleting underlying content.
Constraints: Keep short (≤10 chars on narrow screens); no trailing period.

Surface: Paywall — trial CTA label, shown above the offer duration line (e.g. "Not ready? Try 3 days for free!").
Type: paragraph (call to action lead-in)
Context: Soft CTA asking the hesitant user to try a free trial instead of subscribing immediately. Concatenated at runtime with the trial offer duration.
Constraints: Keep short. Question mark required to mirror the asking tone.
Tone: encouraging.

Surface: Paywall / subscription screen — primary CTA button.
Type: button label
Context: Imperative verb confirming subscription purchase — main paywall CTA when no free trial is offered. Use the same imperative verb form as on the App Store subscription CTA in the target language.
Constraints: Keep short; fits a primary button (typically <=14 chars).

Surface: Create / edit beverage screen — toggle button below the form when editing an existing beverage; hides the drink from the main catalog (it appears under "Hidden drinks" section).
Type: button label
Context: Imperative verb. Action that removes a beverage from the visible catalog while keeping its data (the user can restore it later).

t.ja: ドリンクを非表示にする

context:
Surface: Create / edit beverage screen — same toggle button as "Hide drink", but shown when the beverage is already hidden; restores it back into the main catalog.
Type: button label
Context: Imperative verb. Action that returns a previously hidden beverage to the visible catalog. Paired with "Hide drink" — the button toggles between the two labels.

Surface: Create / edit drink screen navigation title; Edit added drink screen navigation title.
Type: screen title
Context: Navigation bar title shown when editing an existing user drink (in settings) or editing an already-added drink record (in day statistics).
Constraints: Keep short to fit the navigation bar; no trailing period.

en: Standard drinks can’t be edited, but you can hide them from the list if you don’t need them.
context:
Surface: Create / edit drink screen — info label shown when the user opens a standard catalog drink for editing.
Type: paragraph (informational)
Context: Explains that built-in default drinks (water, coffee, tea, etc.) cannot be modified; suggests hiding the drink from the list if not needed.

t.ar: لا يُمكن تعديل المشروبات القياسية. ولكن يُمكنك إخفاءها من القائمة إذا كنت لا تحتاج إليها.
t.da: Standarddrikke kan ikke ændres, men du kan skjule dem fra listen, hvis du ikke har brug for dem.
t.de: Standardgetränke können nicht bearbeitet werden. Wenn du ein Getränk nicht brauchst, kannst du es jedoch aus der Liste ausblenden.
t.en: Standard drinks can’t be edited, but you can hide them from the list if you don’t need them.
t.es: No puedes editar las bebidas estándar, pero puedes ocultarlas de la lista si no las necesitas.
t.fr: Les boissons standard ne peuvent pas être modifiées, mais tu peux les cacher de la liste si tu n'en as pas besoin.
t.hi: स्टैंडर्ड पेय एडिट नहीं किए जा सकते, पर ज़रूरत न हो तो उन्हें सूची से छिपा सकते हो।
t.id: Minuman standar tidak bisa diedit. Tapi kamu bisa menyembunyikannya dari daftar kalau tidak butuh.
t.it: Le bevande standard non possono essere modificate. Ma puoi nasconderle dall'elenco se non ne hai bisogno.
t.ja: 標準的なドリンクは編集できません。ただし、不要ならリストから非表示にできます。
t.ko: 기본 음료는 편집할 수 없지만, 필요하지 않으면 목록에서 숨길 수 있어요.
t.ms: Minuman standard tidak boleh diedit. Tapi awak boleh menyembunyikannya daripada senarai jika awak tidak memerlukannya.
t.nb: Standarddrikker kan ikke redigeres, men du kan skjule dem fra listen hvis du ikke trenger dem.
t.nl: Standaard drankjes kunnen niet worden bewerkt, maar je kunt ze verbergen als je ze niet nodig hebt.
t.pl: Standardowe napoje nie mogą być edytowane. Możesz jednak ukryć je z listy, jeśli nie są ci potrzebne.
t.pt_BR: Bebidas padrão não podem ser editadas. Mas você pode ocultá-las da lista se não precisar delas.
t.ru: Стандартный напиток нельзя редактировать. Но ты можешь скрыть его из списка, если он не нужен.
t.sv: Standarddrycker kan inte redigeras. Men du kan dölja den från listan om du inte behöver den.
t.tr: Standart içecekler düzenlenemez, ihtiyacın yoksa listeden gizleyebilirsin.
t.vi: Không thể chỉnh sửa đồ uống tiêu chuẩn. Nhưng bạn có thể ẩn nó khỏi danh sách nếu không cần đến.
t.zh_CN: 标准饮品无法编辑。但如果你不需要该饮品，可以将它从列表中隐藏。

context:
Surface: Create / edit beverage screen — caffeine and sugar amount-per-volume hint, shown in parentheses next to the section title.
Type: paragraph (unit description)
Context: Generic "<unit> per <volume>" template — describes how much caffeine or sugar a given volume of the beverage contains (currently "mg per 100 ml" / "g per 100 ml"). The reference volume is hardcoded to 100 in the call site; only the unit string is localized.
Placeholders:
  [%1$s] — substance unit, localized via `R.string.localizable.mg()` / `.g()`
  [%2$s] — reference volume composed at the call site as `"100 " + R.string.localizable.ml()`; does not switch to fl oz for imperial users

en: You can't hide the last drink
context:
Surface: Settings → Create / edit drink screen — alert shown when the user tries to hide the last remaining visible beverage in their drinks catalog.
Type: error message
Context: Prevents the user from ending up with zero visible beverages by hiding the only remaining one. "Hide" here is the in-app action (the beverage is not deleted permanently — it can be unhidden later). Despite the key name containing "Deletion", the action is hiding, not deleting; translator should follow the value ("hide"), not the key name.

t.da: Du kan ikke skjule den sidste drik
t.de: Du kannst das letzte Getränk nicht ausblenden
t.en: You can't hide the last drink
t.es: No puedes ocultar la última bebida
t.fr: Tu ne peux pas masquer la dernière boisson
t.hi: आख़िरी पेय छिपाया नहीं जा सकता
t.ja: 最後のドリンクは非表示にできません
t.ms: Awak tidak boleh menyembunyikan minuman terakhir
t.nb: Du kan ikke skjule den siste drikken
t.nl: Je kunt het laatste drankje niet verbergen
t.pt_BR: Não dá para esconder a última bebida
t.sv: Du kan inte dölja den sista drycken
t.vi: Bạn không thể ẩn đồ uống cuối
t.zh_CN: 你无法隐藏最后一款饮品

en: Each drink has a different hydration percentage and affects your body’s water balance differently. You can find out a drink’s ratio by consulting a nutritionist or searching online.
context:
Surface: Create / edit drink screen — explainer popup body shown when the user taps the info button next to the "Water balance" / hydration-coefficient field (CreateDrinkViewController showWaterBalanceAlert). Paired title "waterBalance".
Type: paragraph (instructional)
Context: Two short paragraphs separated by a blank line. Explains that different beverages contribute differently to body water balance (hydration percentage / coefficient varies by drink) and advises the user where to look up a drink's coefficient (nutritionist or Internet).

t.ar: لكل مشروب نسبة ترطيب مختلفة ويؤثر على التوازن المائي للجسم بطرق مختلفة. يُمكنك معرفة نسبة ترطيب المشروب عن طريق استشارة خبير تغذية أو البحث عبر الإنترنت.
t.da: Hver drik har sin egen hydreringsprocent og påvirker kroppens vandbalance forskelligt. Du kan finde en driks forhold ved at rådføre dig med en ernæringsekspert eller søge information online.
t.de: Jedes Getränk hat einen anderen Flüssigkeitsanteil und beeinflusst den Wasserhaushalt des Körpers auf unterschiedliche Weise. Den Koeffizienten eines Getränks kannst du herausfinden, indem du einen Ernährungsberater fragst oder online nachschaust.
t.en: Each drink has a different hydration percentage and affects your body’s water balance differently. You can find out a drink’s ratio by consulting a nutritionist or searching online.
t.es: Cada bebida tiene un porcentaje de hidratación diferente y afecta de forma distinta al equilibrio hídrico del organismo. Puedes consultar a un nutricionista o investigar en Internet para averiguar la proporción de la bebida.
t.fr: Chaque boisson possède un pourcentage d’hydratation spécifique et affecte différemment l’équilibre hydrique du corps. Tu peux connaître le taux propre à cette boisson en consultant un nutritionniste ou en faisant des recherches en ligne.
t.hi: हर पेय का हाइड्रेशन प्रतिशत अलग होता है और यह शरीर के पानी के संतुलन को अलग-अलग तरह से प्रभावित करता है। तुम पोषण विशेषज्ञ से सलाह लेकर या इंटरनेट पर देखकर पेय का अनुपात पता कर सकते हो।
t.id: Setiap minuman memiliki persentase hidrasi berbeda-beda dan memengaruhi keseimbangan air tubuh dengan cara yang berbeda. Kamu bisa mengetahui rasio minuman dengan berkonsultasi kepada ahli gizi atau mencari informasi di Internet.
t.it: Ogni bevanda ha una diversa percentuale di idratazione e influisce in modo diverso sul bilancio idrico del corpo. Puoi scoprire l'indice della bevanda consultando un nutrizionista o cercando informazioni su Internet.
t.ja: ドリンクはそれぞれ水分の割合が異なり、体の水分バランスに与える影響も異なります。栄養士に相談したり、インターネットで情報を検索したりすれば、ドリンクの比率を知ることができます。
t.ko: 음료마다 수분 흡수율이 다르고 체내 수분 균형에 미치는 영향도 달라요. 영양사와 상담하거나 인터넷에서 음료의 수분 흡수율을 찾아볼 수 있어요.
t.ms: Setiap minuman mempunyai peratusan penghidratan yang berbeza dan memberi kesan kepada keseimbangan air badan dengan cara yang berbeza. Awak boleh mengetahui nisbah minuman dengan berunding dengan pakar pemakanan atau mencari maklumat di Internet.
t.nb: Hver drikk har sin egen hydreringsprosent og påvirker kroppens vannbalanse på ulike måter. Du kan finne ut drikkens prosent ved å spørre en ernæringsfysiolog eller søke på nettet.
t.nl: Elk drankje heeft een ander hydratatiepercentage en beïnvloedt de waterbalans van het lichaam op verschillende manieren. Je kunt de verhouding van het drankje achterhalen door een voedingsdeskundige te raadplegen of informatie op internet te zoeken.
t.pl: Każdy napój ma inny procent nawodnienia i w różny sposób wpływa na bilans wodny organizmu. Możesz dowiedzieć się, jaki jest procent dla danego napoju, konsultując się z dietetykiem lub wyszukując informacji w Internecie.
t.pt_BR: Cada bebida tem uma porcentagem de hidratação diferente e afeta o balanço hídrico do corpo de formas distintas. Você pode descobrir a proporção da bebida conversando com um nutricionista ou procurando informações na internet.
t.ru: Каждый напиток имеет свой процент гидратации и по-разному влияет на водный баланс организма. Узнать коэффициент напитка можно у диетолога или в интернете.
t.sv: Alla drycker har olika vattenprocent och påverkar kroppens vattenbalans på olika sätt. Du kan ta reda på dryckens andel genom att fråga en näringsexpert eller hitta information på internet.
t.tr: Her içeceğin farklı bir hidrasyon yüzdesi vardır ve vücudunun su dengesini farklı etkiler. Bir beslenme uzmanına danışarak ya da internette araştırarak içeceğin oranını öğrenebilirsin.
t.vi: Mỗi đồ uống có tỷ lệ cấp nước khác nhau và ảnh hưởng đến cân bằng nước của cơ thể theo những cách khác nhau. Bạn có thể tìm hiểu tỷ lệ của đồ uống bằng cách hỏi ý kiến chuyên gia dinh dưỡng hoặc tìm thông tin trên Internet.
t.zh_CN: 每种饮品的含水量不同，也会以不同方式影响身体的水分平衡。你可以咨询营养师或在线查找资料，了解某种饮品的补水比例。

en: Track the amount of caffeine in your drink. Caffeine in mg per [%s].
context:
Surface: Create / edit drink screen — explainer popup body shown when the user taps the info button next to the caffeine field (CreateDrinkViewController showCaffeineAlert).
Type: paragraph (instructional)
Context: Two short sentences. First explains the purpose of the caffeine field (it lets the user track caffeine intake); second clarifies that the entered value is in milligrams per the given reference volume (e.g. "per 100 ml").
Placeholders:
  [%s] — reference volume with unit, currently assembled at the call site as localized "100 ml"
Constraints: Trailing period on the second sentence.

t.ar: تتبع كمية الكافيين في مشروبك. كمية الكافيين بالملغ لكل [%s].
t.da: Hold øje med koffeinmængden. Mængden af koffein i mg per [%s].
t.de: Behalte den Überblick über die Koffeinmenge. Die Menge an Koffein in mg pro [%s].
t.en: Track the amount of caffeine in your drink. Caffeine in mg per [%s].
t.es: Lleva un registro de la cantidad de cafeína. La cantidad de cafeína en mg por [%s].
t.fr: Suis la quantité de caféine. La quantité de caféine est exprimée en mg par [%s].
t.hi: कैफीन की मात्रा का ध्यान रखो। प्रति [%s] मिलीग्राम में कैफीन।
t.id: Lacak jumlah kafein. Jumlah kafein dalam mg per [%s].
t.it: Tieni traccia della quantità di caffeina. La quantità di caffeina in mg per [%s].
t.ja: カフェイン量を把握しましょう。カフェイン量はmg/[%s]です。
t.ko: 음료에 들어 있는 카페인 양을 기록하세요. 카페인은 [%s]당 mg 단위로 입력해요.
t.ms: Jejak jumlah kafein. Jumlah kafein dalam mg setiap [%s].
t.nb: Hold styr på koffeinmengden. Mengden av koffein i mg per [%s].
t.nl: Houd de hoeveelheid cafeïne bij. De hoeveelheid cafeïne in mg per [%s].
t.pl: Zwracaj uwagę na ilość kofeiny. Ilość kofeiny w mg na [%s].
t.pt_BR: Acompanhe a quantidade de cafeína. A quantidade de cafeína em mg por [%s].
t.ru: Отслеживай количество кофеина в напитке. Кофеин в мг на [%s].
t.sv: Håll koll på mängden koffein. Mängden koffein i mg per [%s].
t.tr: İçeceğindeki kafein miktarını takip et. [%s] başına mg cinsinden kafein.
t.vi: Theo dõi lượng caffeine. Lượng caffeine tính bằng mg mỗi [%s].
t.zh_CN: 记录咖啡因的含量。每 [%s] 所含咖啡因的毫克数。

en: Create your own drinks and customize their properties
context:
Surface: Paywall / subscription benefits list — subtitle under the "Own drinks" benefit item.
Type: paragraph (benefit description)
Context: Describes the premium benefit of creating personalized custom drinks with adjustable properties (color, name, hydration coefficient, caffeine, sugar). Paired with the "Own drinks" title.

t.da: Lav dine egne drikke og tilpas deres egenskaber
t.de: Erstelle deine eigenen Getränke und passe deren Eigenschaften an
t.en: Create your own drinks and customize their properties
t.es: Crea tus propias bebidas y personaliza sus propiedades
t.fr: Crée tes propres boissons et personnalise leurs propriétés
t.hi: अपने खुद के पेय बनाओ और उनके गुण कस्टमाइज़ करो
t.id: Buat minumanmu sendiri dan sesuaikan propertinya
t.it: Crea le tue bevande e personalizza le loro proprietà
t.ja: 自分だけのドリンクを作って成分をカスタマイズしましょう
t.ko: 나만의 음료를 만들고 속성을 자유롭게 설정해 보세요
t.ms: Cipta minuman awak sendiri dan sesuaikan kandungannya
t.nb: Lag dine egne drikker og tilpass egenskapene deres.
t.nl: Maak je eigen drankjes en pas hun eigenschappen aan
t.pl: Twórz własne napoje i dobieraj ich właściwości
t.pt_BR: Crie suas próprias bebidas e personalize as propriedades delas
t.ru: Создавай собственные напитки и настраивай их параметры
t.sv: Skapa egna drycker och anpassa deras egenskaper
t.tr: Kendi içeceklerini yarat ve özelliklerini özelleştir
t.vi: Tạo đồ uống của riêng bạn và tùy chỉnh thuộc tính của chúng
t.zh_CN: 创建你自己的饮料并自定义其属性

context:
Surface: Paywall / subscription benefits list — title of the "create your own drinks" premium benefit item; subscription compare table row; onboarding subscription card.
Type: benefit title
Context: Title of the premium benefit that lets the user create custom personalized drinks. Paired with the subtitle "Create your own drinks and customize their properties".
Constraints: Keep short to fit benefit row title; no trailing period.

en: You can now create your own drinks with My Water Premium! Just go to Settings and open the "[%s]" section.
context:
Surface: Currently no live Swift call-sites (orphan key — legacy onboarding / upsell explainer for the own-drinks premium feature).
Type: paragraph (feature explainer)
Context: Tells the user that after activating My Water Premium they can create custom drinks, and directs them to the settings section whose localized name is interpolated as the placeholder.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — localized name of the settings section that opens the own-drinks editor (e.g. "Own drinks")
Tone: encouraging.

t.ar: يُمكنك الآن صنع مشروباتك المخصصة باستخدام My Water Premium! لتتمكن من ذلك، اذهب إلى إعدادات التطبيق واختر قسم "[%s]".
t.da: Du kan nu lave dine egne drikke med My Water Premium! Gå til Indstillinger, og åbn afsnittet "[%s]".
t.de: Mit My Water Premium kannst du jetzt deine eigenen Getränke erstellen! Öffne dazu die App-Einstellungen und wähle den Bereich „[%s]“ aus.
t.en: You can now create your own drinks with My Water Premium! Just go to Settings and open the "[%s]" section.
t.es: Ya puedes crear tus propias bebidas con My Water Premium. Para hacerlo, ve a los ajustes de la app y selecciona la sección "[%s]".
t.fr: Avec My Water Premium, tu peux maintenant créer tes propres boissons ! Pour cela, va dans les paramètres de l'appli, puis dans la section "[%s]".
t.hi: My Water Premium के साथ अब तुम अपने खुद के पेय बना सकते हो! इसके लिए ऐप की सेटिंग में जाओ और "[%s]" सेक्शन चुनो.
t.id: Sekarang kamu bisa membuat minuman sendiri dengan My Water Premium! Untuk melakukannya, buka pengaturan aplikasi dan pilih bagian "[%s]".
t.it: Ora puoi creare le tue bevande con My Water Premium! Per fare ciò, vai alle impostazioni dell'applicazione e seleziona la sezione "[%s]".
t.ja: 自分だけのドリンクをMy Water Premiumで作れるようになりました！ドリンクを作るには、アプリ設定に進んで「[%s]」の項目を選択してください。
t.ko: 이제 My Water Premium으로 나만의 음료를 만들 수 있어요! 만들려면 앱 설정으로 가서 "[%s]" 섹션을 선택하세요.
t.ms: Awak kini boleh mencipta minuman awak sendiri dengan My Water Premium! Untuk melakukan ini, pergi ke tetapan aplikasi dan pilih bahagian "[%s]".
t.nb: Du kan nå lage dine egne drikker med My Water Premium! Gå til appinnstillingene og velg "[%s]".
t.nl: Je kunt nu je eigen drankjes maken met My Water Premium! Ga hiervoor naar de app-instellingen en selecteer de sectie "[%s]".
t.pl: Dzięki aplikacji My Water Premium możesz teraz tworzyć własne napoje! Aby to zrobić, przejdź do ustawień aplikacji i wybierz sekcję "[%s]".
t.pt_BR: Agora você pode criar suas próprias bebidas com o My Water Premium! Para isso, acesse as configurações do aplicativo e selecione a seção "[%s]".
t.ru: Теперь с My Water Premium можно создавать свои собственные напитки! Для этого зайди в настройки приложения и выбери раздел "[%s]".
t.sv: Nu kan du skapa dina egna drycker med Mitt Vatten Premium! För att göra det, gå till appens inställningar och välj avsnittet "[%s]".
t.tr: My Water Premium ile artık kendi içeceklerini yaratabilirsin! Bunu yapmak için uygulama ayarlarına gir ve "[%s]" bölümünü seç.
t.vi: Giờ đây, bạn có thể tạo đồ uống của riêng mình với My Water Premium! Để làm điều này, hãy đi đến cài đặt ứng dụng và chọn phần "[%s]".
t.zh_CN: 你现在可以使用 My Water Premium 创建自己的饮品！要创建，请前往应用设置，然后选择"[%s]"版块。

context:
Surface: Main screen — discount offer cell title; paywall / subscription system-style header cell title; discount-offer popup title.
Type: paragraph (marketing claim)
Context: Personal discount offer headline — promotes a percentage discount on the premium subscription (e.g. "Special discount -50% for you!"). Often paired with the subtitle "Start drink more water...".
Placeholders:
  [%s] — discount percentage with sign (e.g. "-50%", "30%")
Tone: encouraging / promotional.

t.fr: Pour toi, une remise spéciale de [%s] !
t.ja: 特別な[%s]割引を用意しました！
t.vi: Ưu đãi đặc biệt [%s] cho bạn!
t.zh_CN: 专为你准备的 [%s] 特别折扣！

en: Start drinking more water. Your body will thank you. We will help!
context:
Surface: Main screen — special discount cell, supporting subtitle text under the discount title (specialDiscountCellTitle).
Type: paragraph (marketing claim)
Context: Friendly inviting subtitle that supports the discount offer — frames the discount as the app helping the user drink more water. Note: the English source has a minor grammar slip ("Start drink" instead of "Start drinking"); render target-language grammar naturally and do not propagate the slip.
Tone: encouraging / friendly.

t.ar: ابدأ بشرب المزيد من الماء. سيشكرك جسمك. سنساعدك!
t.da: Begynd at drikke mere vand. Din krop vil takke dig. Vi vil hjælpe dig!
t.de: Fang an, mehr Wasser zu trinken. Dein Körper wird es dir danken. Wir helfen dir dabei!
t.en: Start drinking more water. Your body will thank you. We will help!
t.es: Comienza a beber más agua. Tu cuerpo te lo agradecerá. ¡Te ayudaremos!
t.fr: Commence à boire plus d'eau. Ton corps te remerciera. On t'aidera !
t.hi: ज़्यादा पानी पीना शुरू करो. तुम्हारा शरीर शुक्रिया कहेगा. हम मदद करेंगे!
t.id: Mulai minum lebih banyak air. Tubuhmu akan berterima kasih. Kami akan membantu!
t.it: Inizia a bere più acqua. Il tuo corpo ti ringrazierà. Ti aiuteremo!
t.ja: もっと水を飲む習慣を始めましょう。体が喜びます。My Water がサポートします！
t.ko: 물을 더 자주 마시면, 몸이 고마워할 거예요. 저희가 도울게요!
t.ms: Mula minum lebih banyak air. Badan awak akan berterima kasih. Kami akan membantu!
t.nb: Begynn å drikke mer vann. Kroppen din vil takke deg. Vi hjelper!
t.nl: Begin met meer water drinken. Je lichaam zal je dankbaar zijn. Wij helpen je!
t.pl: Zacznij pić więcej wody. Twoje ciało ci podziękuje. Pomożemy!
t.pt_BR: Comece a beber mais água. Seu corpo vai agradecer. A gente ajuda!
t.ru: Начни пить больше воды. Твоё тело отблагодарит тебя. Мы поможем!
t.sv: Börja dricka mer vatten. Din kropp kommer att tacka dig. Vi hjälper dig!
t.tr: Daha fazla su içmeye başla. Vücudun sana teşekkür edecek. Sana yardımcı olacağız!
t.vi: Hãy bắt đầu uống nhiều nước hơn. Cơ thể sẽ cảm ơn bạn. Chúng tôi sẽ giúp bạn!
t.zh_CN: 开始多喝水。你的身体会感谢你。我们来帮你！

context:
Surface: Progress / activity indicator label (currently no live Swift call-sites — preserved for legacy / pending sync UI).
Type: paragraph (status message)
Context: Tells the user that the app is currently synchronizing data with the server / cloud. Present-continuous status framing ("in progress").
Constraints: Keep concise — typically rendered next to a spinner.

t.vi: Đang đồng bộ hóa dữ liệu

en: You need an active My Water Premium subscription to create or edit your own drinks
context:
Surface: Settings → My drinks → drink editor (Create / Edit drink screen) — premium-gate footnote shown under the form when the user does not have an active premium subscription and the drink is a custom (user-created) beverage.
Type: paragraph (paywall gate notice)
Context: Explains that editing or creating custom (user-defined) beverages requires an active My Water Premium subscription. Shown instead of the regular "default beverage cannot be edited" line. Paired sibling key: "defaultDrinkNoEdit" (the non-premium standard-drink variant).
Constraints: 1 short sentence; the slash-pair "edit/create" can be re-ordered in target language to read naturally.
Tone: informative, not aggressive.

t.ar: أنت بحاجة إلى اشتراك My Water Premium نشط لإنشاء أو تعديل مشروباتك الخاصة
t.da: Du skal have et aktivt My Water Premium-abonnement for at oprette eller redigere dine egne drikkevarer
t.de: Du benötigst ein aktives My Water Premium-Abo, um deine eigenen Getränke zu bearbeiten/erstellen
t.en: You need an active My Water Premium subscription to create or edit your own drinks
t.es: Necesitas una suscripción activa a My Water Premium para editar o crear tus propias bebidas
t.fr: Tu dois avoir un abonnement actif à My Water Premium pour modifier ou créer tes propres boissons.
t.hi: अपने खुद के पेय बनाने या एडिट करने के लिए My Water Premium सदस्यता ज़रूरी है
t.id: Kamu perlu langganan My Water Premium aktif untuk membuat atau mengedit minumanmu sendiri
t.it: È necessario un abbonamento attivo a My Water Premium per modificare/creare le proprie bevande
t.ja: マイドリンクを編集・作成するには、My Water Premium の有効なサブスクリプションが必要です
t.ko: 나만의 음료를 만들거나 편집하려면 My Water Premium 구독이 필요해요.
t.ms: Awak perlukan langganan My Water Premium yang aktif untuk membuat atau mengedit minuman awak sendiri
t.nb: Du trenger et aktivt My Water Premium-abonnement for å lage eller redigere dine egne drikker
t.nl: Je hebt een actief My Water Premium-abonnement nodig om je eigen drankjes te bewerken/aan te maken
t.pl: Musisz mieć aktywną subskrypcję My Water Premium, aby móc edytować/tworzyć własne napoje.
t.pt_BR: Você precisa de uma assinatura My Water Premium ativa para editar/criar suas próprias bebidas
t.ru: Редактирование и создание своих напитков доступно с подпиской My Water Premium.
t.sv: Du måste ha en aktiv My Water Premium-prenumeration för att redigera/skapa dina egna drycker.
t.tr: Kendi içeceklerini düzenlemek/oluşturmak için aktif bir My Water Premium aboneliğine ihtiyacın var
t.vi: Bạn cần đăng ký My Water Premium đang hoạt động để chỉnh sửa / tạo đồ uống của riêng mình
t.zh_CN: 需要有效的 My Water Premium 订阅才能编辑/创建你自己的饮料

context:
Surface: Paywall — badge / label on a subscription option showing the discount percentage.
Type: badge / label
Context: Short badge showing how much the user saves on the subscription (e.g. "50% off!", "30% off!"). Shown on yearly / discounted tier buttons.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — discount percent, already formatted with percent sign (e.g. "50%", "30%")
Constraints: Keep very short — fits a compact badge.

Surface: Apphud rule-based push notification, title.
Type: notification title
Context: Title of an Apphud server-driven push sent when the user has signaled dissatisfaction with the app (e.g. low rating prompt). Opens a follow-up feedback flow. Phrased as a soft rhetorical question.
Register: casual T-form — push reminder titles use casual partner voice (default for non-formal surfaces).

t.ja: アプリは気に入りませんでしたか？
t.nl: Vind je de app niet leuk?

en: Help us improve. Tell us what’s not working for you.
context:
Surface: Push notification / Apphud rule-based feedback prompt (currently unreferenced in Swift code, preserved for legacy / Apphud-server-driven push text).
Type: notification body
Context: Follow-up message after the user signaled the app is not helping (see "didntLikeAppApphudPush"); asks for actionable feedback on what's wrong and how to improve.
Register: casual T-form — push reminders use casual partner voice (default for non-formal surfaces).
Tone: neutral / informative — direct request, not accusatory.

t.ar: ساعدنا على التحسّن. أخبرنا بما لا يعمل عندك.
t.da: Hjælp os med at blive bedre. Fortæl os, hvad der ikke fungerer for dig.
t.de: Hilf uns, besser zu werden. Sag uns, was bei dir nicht funktioniert.
t.en: Help us improve. Tell us what’s not working for you.
t.es: Cuéntanos qué no te funciona. ¿Cómo podemos mejorar?
t.fr: Aide-nous à nous améliorer. Dis-nous ce qui ne fonctionne pas pour toi.
t.hi: हमें बेहतर बनने में मदद करो। बताओ क्या काम नहीं कर रहा।
t.id: Bantu kami jadi lebih baik. Beri tahu kami apa yang tidak berfungsi untukmu.
t.it: Aiutaci a migliorare. Dicci cosa non funziona per te.
t.ja: もっと良いアプリにするため、うまくいかない点を教えてください。
t.ko: 더 나아지도록 도와주세요. 무엇이 잘 안 되는지 알려주세요.
t.ms: Bantu kami jadi lebih baik. Beritahu kami apa yang tak berfungsi untuk awak.
t.nb: Hjelp oss å bli bedre. Fortell oss hva som ikke fungerer for deg.
t.nl: Help ons beter te worden. Vertel ons wat er voor jou niet werkt.
t.pl: Pomóż nam się rozwijać. Powiedz, co ci nie działa.
t.pt_BR: Ajude-nos a melhorar. Conte o que não está funcionando para você.
t.ru: Помоги нам стать лучше. Расскажи, что не работает.
t.sv: Hjälp oss att bli bättre. Berätta vad som inte fungerar för dig.
t.tr: Daha iyi olmamıza yardım et. Neyin işe yaramadığını bize anlat.
t.vi: Hãy cho chúng tôi biết điều gì chưa ổn để chúng tôi cải thiện nhé.
t.zh_CN: 帮助我们改进。请告诉我们哪里对你不好用。

context:
Surface: Sign-in / registration screens — prompt next to the "Sign in" link.
Type: paragraph (prompt)
Context: Question shown next to a "Sign in" link to nudge already-registered users to switch from the registration flow to the sign-in flow.

t.ja: すでにアカウントをお持ちの方

en: Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
context:
Surface: Password reset screen — instructional description paragraph above the email input field.
Type: paragraph
Context: Tells the user to enter their account email so the app can send a password reset link to it.

t.ar: أدخل عنوان البريد الإلكتروني لحسابك في التطبيق. سنرسل لك رابطا لإعادة تعيين كلمة المرور الخاصة بك.
t.da: Indtast e-mailadressen til din konto i appen. Vi sender dig et link til at nulstille din adgangskode.
t.de: Gib die E-Mail-Adresse deines App-Kontos ein. Wir senden dir einen Link, um dein Passwort zurückzusetzen.
t.en: Enter your email address and we'll send you a link to reset your password.
t.es: Introduce la dirección de correo electrónico de tu cuenta. Te enviaremos un enlace para restablecer tu contraseña.
t.fr: Saisis l'adresse e-mail de ton compte dans l'application. Nous t'enverrons un lien pour réinitialiser ton mot de passe.
t.hi: अपने खाते का ईमेल डालो। हम तुम्हें पासवर्ड रीसेट करने का लिंक भेजेंगे।
t.id: Masukkan alamat email akunmu di aplikasi. Kami akan mengirim tautan untuk mengatur ulang kata sandimu.
t.it: Inserisci l'indirizzo email del tuo account. Ti invieremo un link per reimpostare la password.
t.ja: アプリに登録されているアカウントのメールアドレスを入力してください。パスワードをリセットするためのリンクをお送りします。
t.ko: 계정의 이메일 주소를 입력해 주세요. 비밀번호 재설정 링크를 보내드릴게요.
t.ms: Masukkan alamat e-mel akaun awak. Kami akan menghantar pautan kepada awak untuk menetapkan semula kata laluan awak.
t.nb: Skriv inn e-postadressen til kontoen din i appen. Vi sender deg en lenke for å tilbakestille passordet ditt.
t.nl: Voer het e-mailadres van je account in. We sturen je een link om je wachtwoord opnieuw in te stellen.
t.pl: Wpisz adres e-mail swojego konta. Wyślemy ci link, aby zresetować hasło.
t.pt_BR: Digite o e-mail da sua conta. Vamos enviar um link para redefinir sua senha.
t.ru: Введи адрес электронной почты аккаунта, и мы отправим тебе ссылку для сброса пароля.
t.sv: Ange e-postadressen till ditt konto i appen. Vi skickar dig en länk för att återställa ditt lösenord.
t.tr: Hesabının e-posta adresini gir. Parolanı sıfırlaman için sana bir bağlantı göndereceğiz.
t.vi: Nhập địa chỉ email tài khoản của bạn vào ứng dụng. Chúng tôi sẽ gửi cho bạn một liên kết để đặt lại mật khẩu của bạn.
t.zh_CN: 在应用程序中输入你的账户的电子邮件地址。我们将向你发送一个链接，以重设你的密码。

en: No drinks added on this day
context:

Tone: neutral.

t.hi: इस दिन कोई पेय नहीं जोड़ा गया

t.it: Nessuna bevanda aggiunta per questo giorno

context:

Context: Noun "Time" used as a field label for the clock time (hh:mm) at which a drink was logged. Distinct from the lowercase "hour" unit and from "selectTime" picker prompt. Title-case for row / nav title use.
Constraints: Keep short (single word where possible).

Surface: Friends list screen — incoming friend request cell, secondary action button (paired with the "Add" accept button).
Type: button label
Context: Imperative verb. Rejects / declines an incoming friend request.
Constraints: Keep short (≤10 chars). No trailing period.

Type: empty state title
Context: Shown when the beverages breakdown chart has no data for the selected period.
Tone: neutral.

t.ja: 追加された飲み物はありません

context:
Surface: Friends list screen — empty state title (no friends added yet).
Type: empty state title
Context: Shown when the user's friends list contains no entries. Paired with the subtitle "noFriends" inviting the user to add friends.
Constraints: Keep short (single line on narrow screens).

t.ja: まだ友だちがいません

context:
Surface: Settings → Friends list screen — empty-state title shown when the friends list fails to load because the device is offline.
Type: screen title
Context: Headline for the no-internet empty state. Paired with the body / subtitle key "connectionErrorSubtitle". Short, single-line.

t.ja: インターネットに接続されていません

en: Please check your connection and try again
context:
Surface: Settings → Friends list screen — empty-state subtitle shown below "connectionErrorTitle" when the friends list fails to load because the device is offline.
Type: paragraph
Context: Action-oriented body asking the user to verify their connection and retry. Paired with the title key "connectionErrorTitle" — translate as a pair so the two lines read coherently. Tone is polite and action-oriented.

t.ar: يرجى التحقق من اتصالك ثم المحاولة مرة أخرى
t.da: Kontroller venligst din forbindelse, og prøv igen
t.de: Bitte überprüfe deine Verbindung und versuche es erneut
t.en: Please check your connection and try again
t.es: Por favor, comprueba tu conexión e inténtalo de nuevo
t.hi: अपना कनेक्शन जाँचो और फिर कोशिश करो
t.ja: 接続を確認して、もう一度お試しください
t.ko: 연결을 확인한 후 다시 시도해 주세요
t.ms: Sila semak sambungan awak dan cuba lagi
t.nl: Controleer je verbinding en probeer het opnieuw
t.pt_BR: Verifique sua conexão e tente de novo
t.ru: Проверь соединение и попробуй ещё раз
t.sv: Kontrollera din anslutning och försök igen
t.tr: Lütfen bağlantını kontrol edip tekrar dene
t.vi: Vui lòng kiểm tra kết nối và thử lại
t.zh_CN: 请检查网络连接后重试

context:
Surface: Onboarding weather / climate selection screen — stage title (`OnboardingWeatherViewController`). Also used as the title of the "Weather" parameter card in the goal-calculation parameters screen (`OnboardingChooseCalculationViewModel`).
Type: screen title / parameter card title
Context: Single-word noun naming the climate / weather parameter that contributes to the daily water goal calculation. The screen lets the user pick between climate options (cold / temperate / hot).
Constraints: Keep short (single word where possible).

Surface: Onboarding weather screen — title of the "normal / temperate climate" option card (paired with subtitle key "normalClimateDescription"; sibling option "hotClimate" / "hotClimateDescription").
Type: option label
Context: Title of the default climate option presented during onboarding — selecting it leaves the daily-goal calculation at its baseline (no hot-climate adjustment). Used only in this climate-selector context, not as a generic settings value (the app-wide "On" / settings "Normal" status is a different key).
Constraints: Keep short — single word fitting an option card title.

Surface: Onboarding weather screen — subtitle under the "Normal" climate option card (paired with title key "normal").
Type: option description
Context: Short description clarifying that the "Normal" option means a temperate climate and is the default choice if the user does not live in a hot region. Sibling of "hotClimateDescription".
Constraints: Keep concise — fits one line under the option title. "(default)" annotation is informational; preserve the parenthetical convention.

t.ja: 温暖な気候 (デフォルト)

context:
Surface: Onboarding weather screen — title of the "hot climate" option card (OnboardingWeatherViewController) paired with subtitle key "hotClimateDescription"; sibling option "normal" / "normalClimateDescription". Affects daily water goal calculation (DailyNormService).
Type: option / card title
Context: Short adjective describing a hot climate region — selected by users living in sunny / hot areas to bump up the recommended daily water amount. Capitalized as a card title.
Constraints: Keep very short (single adjective, ≤8 chars).

en: Lots of sunny and hot days
context:
Surface: Onboarding weather screen — subtitle under the "hotClimate" option card (OnboardingWeatherViewController). Sibling of "normalClimateDescription".
Type: option / card description
Context: One-line description clarifying that the "Hot" option means many sunny and hot days. Sits under the "Hot" title in the climate selection card.
Constraints: Keep concise — fits one line under the option title.

t.da: Masser af solrige og varme dage
t.en: Lots of sunny and hot days
t.es: Muchos días de sol y calor
t.fr: Beaucoup de journées chaudes et ensoleillées
t.hi: बहुत ज़्यादा धूप और गर्मी के दिन
t.id: Banyak hari yang cerah dan panas
t.ms: Banyak hari yang cerah dan panas
t.zh_CN: 有很多晴朗炎热的日子

context:
Surface: Onboarding drinks survey — screen stage title.
Type: screen title
Context: Title of the onboarding step where the user selects which sugary drinks (soda, sweet juices, etc.) they consume. Drives initial drink-habits profile.
Constraints: Keep short to fit the stage title area; no trailing period.

Surface: Onboarding "Sugary beverages" and "Coffee and energy drinks" questionnaire screens — frequency option title (selectable card / row).
Type: option title
Context: Lowest-frequency option in the "how often do you consume X" picker (paired with `almostNeverSubtitle`). Used both for sugary drinks (`OnboardingDrinksViewModel`) and for coffee / energy drinks (`OnboardingCoffeeViewModel`) — affects daily water goal calculation.
Constraints: Keep short (frequency option card title).

Surface: Onboarding "Sugary beverages" and "Coffee and energy drinks" questionnaire screens — description shown under the "Almost never" option title.
Type: option subtitle
Context: Clarifies what frequency "Almost never" actually means in this questionnaire (essentially zero up to a few times per month). The "/" separates two equivalent example frequencies.
Constraints: Single short line; fits as subtitle under an option title.

t.hi: कभी नहीं / महीने में कई बार
t.id: Tidak pernah / beberapa kali dalam sebulan
t.ko: 전혀 안 함 / 한 달에 몇 번
t.vi: Không bao giờ / vài lần một tháng

context:
Surface: Onboarding "Sugary beverages", "Coffee and energy drinks", and fruits / vegetables questionnaire screens — description shown under the "Rarely" option title.
Type: option subtitle
Context: Clarifies what frequency "Rarely" means in these questionnaires (low-frequency weekly consumption). Used for sugary drinks (`OnboardingDrinksViewModel`), coffee / energy drinks (`OnboardingCoffeeViewModel`), and fruits / vegetables (`OnboardingFruitsViewController`) — affects daily water goal calculation.
Constraints: Single short line; fits as subtitle under an option title.

Surface: Onboarding drinks / coffee / fruits questionnaire option subtitle for "daily" frequency, and Settings → Notifications repeat-days summary when reminders fire every day of the week.
Type: option subtitle / settings value
Context: Time-frequency label meaning "occurs each day". In onboarding it sits under an option title as the example frequency for drinks / coffee / fruits, not the Activity workout screen. In notifications settings it shows as the current value when all 7 weekdays are selected.
Constraints: Keep short — fits as a subtitle / value on one line.

Surface: Onboarding questionnaire option subtitle for "often" frequency — sugary drinks (`OnboardingDrinksViewModel`), coffee / energy drinks (`OnboardingCoffeeViewModel`), and fruits / vegetables (`OnboardingFruitsViewController`) screens.
Type: option subtitle
Context: Clarifies what frequency "Often" means in these questionnaires — multiple servings within a single day. Sibling of "almostNeverSubtitle" / "rarelySubtitle" / "everyDay".
Constraints: Single short line; fits as subtitle under an option title.

Surface: Onboarding "Coffee and energy drinks" questionnaire screen (OnboardingCoffeeViewController) — stage title above the frequency options.
Type: screen title
Context: Title of the onboarding step where the user picks how often they drink coffee and energy drinks (affects daily water goal — caffeine has a mild diuretic effect). Plural "drinks" applies to both coffee and energy beverages.

t.ja: コーヒーとエナジードリンク
t.vi: Cà phê và nước tăng lực

context:
Surface: Onboarding "Vegetables and fruits" questionnaire screen (`OnboardingFruitsViewController`) — stage title above the frequency options.
Type: screen title
Context: Title of the onboarding step where the user picks how often they eat vegetables and fruits (affects daily water goal — produce contains water and contributes to hydration). Key has historical spaces in identifier; R.swift exposes it as `vegetablesAndFruits()`. Do not rename the key.
Constraints: Keep short to fit the stage title area; no trailing period.

Surface: Settings → user profile row — colored CTA shown to logged-out users (replaces the user name).
Type: button label
Context: Tappable CTA opening the authorization flow (sign-in or sign-up). Covers both actions in one label since the destination screen offers both options.
Constraints: Keep reasonably short — fits a single row in Settings; long translations may wrap.

t.hi: साइन इन या साइन अप करो
t.ja: サインインまたは登録

en: Based on your parameters, your estimated goal is [%1$.1f] [%2$s] of water. Your doctor can determine the exact goal for you.
context:
Surface: Onboarding goal-calculation result screen (`OnboardingNormViewModel`, `OnboardingNormCircleViewController`) — shown for fractional units like litres.
Type: paragraph (educational)
Context: Explains the personalized daily water goal computed from the user's parameters; suggests consulting a doctor for the exact value. Fractional-digit variant (used for litres). Compare `youShouldDrink0Digits` for whole-number variant (ml / fl oz).
Placeholders:
  [%1$.1f] — daily goal amount with 1 decimal (e.g. 1.2)
  [%2$s] — measurement unit ("l" / "ml" / "fl oz")

t.ar: بناءً على معاييرك، الكمية المستهدفة من الماء هي [%1$.1f] [%2$s] تقريبًا. ويمكن لطبيبك تحديد الكمية الدقيقة المناسبة لك.
t.da: Baseret på dine parametre er dit estimerede mål [%1$.1f] [%2$s] vand. Din læge kan fastslå dit nøjagtige mål.
t.de: Basierend auf deinen Angaben liegt dein geschätztes Ziel bei [%1$.1f] [%2$s] Wasser. Dein Arzt kann das genaue Ziel für dich bestimmen.
t.en: Based on your parameters, your estimated goal is [%1$.1f] [%2$s] of water. Your doctor can determine the exact goal for you.
t.es: Según tus datos, la meta estimada es [%1$.1f] [%2$s] de agua. Tu médico puede determinar tu meta exacta.
t.fr: L'objectif estimé en fonction de tes paramètres est [%1$.1f] [%2$s] d'eau. Ton médecin peut déterminer l'objectif exact pour toi.
t.hi: तुम्हारे पैरामीटर के आधार पर पानी का अनुमानित लक्ष्य [%1$.1f] [%2$s] है. तुम्हारे डॉक्टर तुम्हारे लिए सटीक लक्ष्य तय कर सकते हैं.
t.id: Perkiraan target berdasarkan parametermu adalah [%1$.1f] [%2$s] air. Doktermu bisa menentukan target pastinya.
t.it: L'obiettivo stimato in base ai tuoi parametri è [%1$.1f] [%2$s] di acqua. Il tuo medico può determinare l'obiettivo esatto per te.
t.ja: 入力した情報から算出した1日の目標水分量の目安は [%1$.1f] [%2$s] です。正確な量については医師にご相談ください。
t.ko: 입력한 정보를 바탕으로 한 예상 목표는 물 [%1$.1f] [%2$s]예요. 정확한 목표는 의사와 상담해 보세요.
t.ms: Berdasarkan parameter awak, sasaran anggaran awak ialah [%1$.1f] [%2$s] air. Doktor awak boleh menentukan sasaran yang tepat untuk awak.
t.nb: Beregnet mål basert på parameterne dine er [%1$.1f] [%2$s] vann. Legen din kan finne det nøyaktige målet for deg.
t.nl: Op basis van je gegevens is je geschatte doel [%1$.1f] [%2$s] water. Je arts kan het exacte doel voor je bepalen.
t.pl: Szacowany cel na podstawie twoich parametrów to [%1$.1f] [%2$s] wody. Twój lekarz może określić dla ciebie dokładny cel.
t.pt_BR: Com base nos seus dados, sua meta estimada é de [%1$.1f] [%2$s] de água. Seu médico pode definir a meta exata para você.
t.ru: Ориентировочная цель на основе твоих параметров: [%1$.1f] [%2$s] воды. Точную цель поможет определить врач.
t.sv: Uppskattat mål baserat på dina parametrar är [%1$.1f] [%2$s] vatten. Din läkare kan fastställa det exakta målet åt dig.
t.tr: Parametrelerine göre tahmini hedef [%1$.1f] [%2$s] su. Doktorun senin için kesin hedefi belirleyebilir.
t.vi: Mục tiêu ước tính dựa trên các thông số của bạn là [%1$.1f] [%2$s] nước. Bác sĩ của bạn có thể xác định mục tiêu chính xác dành cho bạn.
t.zh_CN: 根据你的参数，预估饮水目标是 [%1$.1f] [%2$s]。医生可以帮你确定更精确的目标。

context:
Surface: Settings screen — "Join community" cell label above the row of MyWater brand social-network icons (Facebook, Instagram, Twitter).
Type: section label
Context: Invitation header introducing the app's official social-network accounts that the user can open from this cell. "Our" = MyWater's (the app's). Title-cased label.
Constraints: Keep short (one line, fits a cell label).

t.vi: Mạng xã hội của chúng tôi

context:
Surface: Onboarding height screen / scale slider — unit toggle button label (shown when metric system is selected).
Type: unit abbreviation
Context: Short abbreviation for "centimeters" — used as the units indicator next to the height slider when the user is in the metric system.
Constraints: Keep abbreviation form (2-3 chars); use locale-standard abbreviation ("см" in ru, "cm" in en/de/fr, etc.).

Surface: Onboarding height screen / scale slider — unit toggle button label (shown when imperial system is selected).
Type: unit abbreviation
Context: Imperial height units indicator — shown next to the height slider when the user is in feet/inches. Compare `centimeters` for metric variant.
Constraints: Short label fitting next to the slider. Translate to the locale-standard imperial height units phrasing.

Surface: Onboarding age screen (`OnboardingAgeViewController`) — stage title above the age picker.
Type: screen title
Context: Prompts the user to pick their age (parameter that contributes to the daily water goal calculation). Note: the key name reads "yourAge" but the English value is "Choose your age" — keep the imperative "choose your …" framing in translation (matches sibling "yourHeight").

Surface: Onboarding height screen — stage title above the height slider; also the height-row label in the shared `ScaleSlider` component (`ScaleSliderViewModel`, `.height` case).
Type: screen title / row label
Context: Prompts the user to pick their height (parameter that contributes to the daily water goal calculation). Note: the key name reads "yourHeight" but the English value is "Choose your height" — keep the imperative "choose your …" framing in translation (matches sibling "yourAge").

Surface: Onboarding name screen (`OnboardingNameViewController`) — stage title, text-field label, accessibility label, and placeholder for the name input.
Type: screen title / field label / placeholder
Context: Single noun phrase used both as the screen title and as the input field placeholder asking the user to enter their first name. Unlike `yourAge` / `yourHeight` (imperative "choose your …"), this one is a noun phrase ("Your name") because it labels a free-text input rather than a picker.

Surface: Onboarding weather / climate selection screen — option title for cold weather.
Type: option title
Context: Selectable climate option meaning "Cold climate"; affects daily water goal calculation. Paired with subtitle "Few sunny days, rainy" (`coldClimateDescription`).
Constraints: Keep short (≤10 chars on narrow screens).

Surface: Onboarding weather / climate selection screen — subtitle under the "Cold" climate option (OnboardingWeatherViewController, paired with the "coldClimate" title).
Type: option subtitle
Context: Short descriptor characterising a cold climate ("few sunny days, rainy"). Two short noun phrases joined by a comma; no verb.
Constraints: Keep concise — fits as a one-line subtitle under the option title.

t.ja: 晴れの日が少なく、雨が多い

t.es: Personaje de la pantalla de inicio
t.ja: ホーム画面のキャラクター

en: Choose a character for your Home screen. It will help you drink more water.
context:
Surface: Settings → Characters picker screen — description paragraph above the sectioned grid of mascots.
Type: paragraph (subtitle)
Context: Explains to the user that the selected character appears on the home screen and motivates them to drink more water.

t.ar: اختر شخصية للشاشة الرئيسية للتطبيق، مما سيساعدك على شرب المزيد من الماء.
t.da: Vælg en karakter til din startskærm, som vil hjælpe dig med at drikke mere vand.
t.de: Wähle einen Charakter für deinen Startbildschirm, der dir hilft, mehr Wasser zu trinken.
t.en: Choose a character for your Home screen. It will help you drink more water.
t.es: Elige un personaje para la pantalla de inicio de la aplicación, que te ayudará a beber más agua.
t.fr: Choisis un personnage pour l'écran d'accueil de l'application, qui t'aidera à boire plus d'eau.
t.hi: होम स्क्रीन के लिए एक कैरेक्टर चुनो, जो तुम्हें ज़्यादा पानी पीने में मदद करेगा।
t.id: Pilih karakter untuk layar Beranda aplikasi yang akan membantumu minum lebih banyak air.
t.it: Scegli un personaggio per la schermata Home. Ti aiuterà a bere più acqua.
t.ja: ホーム画面に表示するキャラクターを選びましょう。もっと水を飲むのに役立ちます。
t.ko: 홈 화면에 표시할 캐릭터를 선택하세요. 물을 더 마시는 데 도움이 돼요.
t.ms: Pilih watak untuk skrin Utama aplikasi, yang akan membantu awak minum lebih banyak air.
t.nb: Velg en figur for startskjermen. Den hjelper deg å drikke mer vann.
t.nl: Kies een personage voor je startscherm. Het helpt je om meer water te drinken.
t.pl: Wybierz postać na ekranie głównym aplikacji, która pomoże ci pić więcej wody.
t.pt_BR: Escolha um personagem para a tela inicial do app. Ele vai ajudar você a beber mais água.
t.ru: Выбери персонажа для главного экрана приложения, который поможет тебе пить больше воды.
t.sv: Välj en karaktär för appens hemskärm, som hjälper dig att dricka mer vatten.
t.tr: Uygulamanın Ana ekranı için daha fazla su içmene yardımcı olacak bir karakter seç.
t.vi: Chọn một nhân vật cho Màn hình chính của ứng dụng, điều này sẽ giúp bạn uống nhiều nước hơn.
t.zh_CN: 为应用程序的主屏幕选择一个角色，这将帮助你喝更多的水。

en: Hi! Now you can choose a character in My Water to help you drink more water. Pick the style you like best!
context:
Surface: Characters feature-introduction popup shown once when the character picker becomes available.
Type: paragraph (feature announcement)
Context: Greets the user and announces that they can now choose a Home screen character (mascot) in My Water that will help them drink more water, and pick the character style they prefer.
Tone: encouraging, friendly.

t.ar: أهلًا! يُمكنك الآن اختيار شخصية في My Water لتساعدك على شرب المزيد من الماء. يُمكنك اختيار مظهر الشخصية بنفسك!
t.da: Hej! Nu kan du vælge en karakter i Mit vand, som hjælper dig med at drikke mere vand. Du kan selv vælge karakterens stil!
t.de: Hallo! Jetzt kannst du in My Water einen Charakter auswählen, der dir hilft, mehr Wasser zu trinken. Du kannst den Stil des Charakters selbst wählen!
t.en: Hi! Now you can choose a character in My Water to help you drink more water. Pick the style you like best!
t.es: ¡Hola! Ahora puedes elegir un personaje en My Water que te ayudará a beber más agua. Puedes seleccionar el estilo de personaje que quieras.
t.fr: Salut ! Tu peux maintenant choisir un personnage dans My Water qui t'aidera à boire plus d'eau. Tu peux choisir le style de personnage que tu veux !
t.hi: नमस्ते! अब तुम My Water में एक कैरेक्टर चुन सकते हो जो तुम्हें ज़्यादा पानी पीने में मदद करेगा। तुम अपने लिए कैरेक्टर की स्टाइल चुन सकते हो!
t.id: Hai! Sekarang kamu bisa memilih karakter di My Water yang akan membantumu minum lebih banyak air. Kamu bisa memilih gaya karaktermu sendiri!
t.it: Ciao! Ora in «La mia acqua» puoi scegliere un personaggio che ti aiuterà a bere più acqua. Puoi scegliere lo stile che preferisci!
t.ja: こんにちは！My Water では、もっと水を飲むのを助けてくれるキャラクターを選べるようになりました。お好みのスタイルを選んでくださいね！
t.ko: 안녕하세요! 이제 «My Water»에서 물을 더 마시도록 도와주는 캐릭터를 고를 수 있어요. 원하는 스타일의 캐릭터를 선택해 보세요!
t.ms: Hai! Kini awak boleh memilih watak dalam My Water yang akan membantu awak minum lebih banyak air. Awak boleh memilih gaya watak sendiri!
t.nb: Hei! Nå kan du velge en figur i Mitt Vann som hjelper deg til å drikke mer vann. Du kan velge din egen figur!
t.nl: Hoi! Je kunt nu in My Water een personage kiezen dat je helpt om meer water te drinken. Je kunt zelf de stijl van het personage kiezen!
t.pl: Cześć! Teraz w aplikacji Moja woda można wybrać postać, która pomoże ci pić więcej wody. Wybierz styl postaci dla siebie!
t.pt_BR: Olá! Agora você pode escolher um personagem no My Water para ajudar você a beber mais água. Você também pode escolher o estilo dele!
t.ru: Привет! Теперь в приложении "Моя вода" появился персонаж, который будет помогать тебе пить больше воды. Ты можешь выбрать персонажа на свой вкус!
t.sv: Hej! Nu kan du välja en karaktär i My Water som hjälper dig att dricka mer vatten. Du kan själv välja stil på karaktären!
t.tr: Merhaba! Artık My Water'da daha fazla su içmene yardımcı olacak bir karakter seçebilirsin. Karakterin stilini kendin seçebilirsin!
t.vi: Xin chào! Bây giờ bạn có thể chọn một nhân vật trong My Water sẽ giúp bạn uống nhiều nước hơn. Bạn có thể chọn phong cách của nhân vật cho mình!
t.zh_CN: 你好！现在你可以在 My Water 里选择一个角色来帮你喝更多的水。你可以自己选择角色的风格！

context:
Surface: app-wide — generic "Select" CTA button used on pickers / option lists where the user confirms a choice (e.g. character picker, drink/icon picker confirmation).
Type: button label
Context: Imperative verb. Confirms the user's selection on the current screen and proceeds. Reusable atom; translate as the equivalent short confirm-selection verb in the target language (not "Choose" — selection is already made; this confirms it).
Constraints: Keep short (1 word, ≤10 chars) — fits a compact CTA button. No trailing period.

en: Tips to help you start drinking more water now

t.da: Tips til at hjælpe dig i gang med at drikke mere vand nu
t.de: Tipps, die dir helfen, jetzt mit dem Wassertrinken anzufangen
t.en: Tips to help you start drinking more water now

t.fr: Des conseils pour t'aider à boire plus d'eau dès maintenant

t.it: Consigli per aiutarti a iniziare a bere più acqua

t.nl: Tips om je te helpen nu meer water te gaan drinken
t.pl: Wskazówki pomocne w piciu większej ilości wody
t.pt_BR: Dicas para ajudar você a beber mais água agora
t.ru: Советы, которые помогут тебе начать пить больше воды прямо сейчас
t.sv: Tips som hjälper dig att börja dricka mer vatten nu

context:
Surface: Weight input controls (onboarding weight step, scale slider, value picker, weight unit labels).
Type: unit abbreviation
Context: Abbreviation for "stone" — British/imperial unit of body weight (1 stone = 14 pounds). Shown alongside the pound abbreviation "lb" when the user selects stones+pounds as the weight unit.
Constraints: Keep very short (1-3 chars); standard locale abbreviation, no period.

Surface: First-launch / Main screen — tutorial coach-mark text pointing at the "+" Add Drink button in the bottom menu / tab bar.
Type: tutorial step
Context: One-step instructional pointer telling new users where to tap to log a drink. The "+" character is the literal plus glyph used in the app's add-drink button — preserve as a literal "+", do not translate to a word.
Constraints: Keep short — fits as a coach-mark callout near the button.
Tone: instructional.

Surface: Main screen — title of the compact weight-tracking promo card linking to the weight tracker.
Type: row title / card title
Context: Short imperative pitch for the weight tracking feature, shown as a card on the Main screen. Singular "dynamic" here means change-over-time / trend.
Constraints: Keep short (compact promo card title).

t.vi: Theo dõi biến động về cân nặng

Tone: playful / promotional.

en: See your [%i] results in My Water!
context:
Surface: Main screen — New Year digest cell title (NewYearDigestCell / DigestCell) inviting the user to open the yearly results recap.
Type: section header / promo card title
Context: Headline of the main-screen card that opens the New Year yearly digest (NewYearDigestViewController). Frames the digest as the user's personal yearly recap (drinks, daily goals, etc.) for the closing year. The [%i] is currently hardcoded to 2023.
Placeholders: [%i] — calendar year as integer (e.g. 2023). Keep numeric formatting; locales should not write the year as words.
Register: casual T-form — main-screen promo card asking the user to look at their personal year; casual companion voice (default for non-formal surfaces).
Tone: encouraging, seasonal.

context:
