t.nb: Slette dette daglige målet fra historikken?
t.nl: Dit dagelijkse doel uit de geschiedenis verwijderen?
t.pl: Usunąć ten dzienny cel z historii?
t.pt_BR: Excluir esta meta diária do histórico?
t.ru: Удалить эту дневную цель из истории?
t.sv: Ta bort det här dagliga målet från historiken?
t.tr: Bu günlük hedefi geçmişten silmek istiyor musun?
t.vi: Xóa mục tiêu hằng ngày này khỏi lịch sử?
t.zh_CN: 从历史中删除这条每日目标？

context:
Surface: "Choose beverage" picker (navigation title in the select-beverage flow), Statistics container tab title, Edit drink screen row title.
Type: screen title / row title
Context: Noun phrase. Refers to which beverage / drink type a logged drink belongs to (water, coffee, juice, etc.). Distinct from "beverage" key which titles the same picker in the create-favorite-drink flow.
Constraints: Keep short.

en: This beverage is already in your favorites
context:
Surface: Drink volume / create-favorite-drink screen — error alert body shown when the user tries to save a favorite beverage that already exists.
Type: error message
Context: Tells the user that the (beverage + volume) combination they tried to save is already in their favorites list.

t.ar: هذا المشروب مضاف إلى المفضلة بالفعل
t.en: This beverage is already in your favorites
t.es: Esta bebida favorita ya está añadida
t.fr: Cette boisson favorite est déjà ajoutée
t.hi: यह पसंदीदा पेय पहले से जोड़ा हुआ है
t.it: Questa bevanda preferita è già stata aggiunta
t.ja: このお気に入りの飲み物はすでに追加されています
t.nb: Denne favorittdrikken er allerede lagt til
t.nl: Dit favoriete drankje is al toegevoegd
t.pl: Ten napój jest już w ulubionych
t.pt_BR: Esta bebida favorita já foi adicionada
t.ru: Этот напиток уже добавлен в избранное
t.sv: Den här favoritdrycken är redan tillagd
t.vi: Đồ uống yêu thích này đã được thêm

en: Couldn't add this favorite beverage. Please try again
context:
Surface: Add drink → drink volume screen — alert message shown when saving a new favorite (beverage + volume) combination to Realm fails (`FavoriteDrinksManager.AddFavoriteDrinkError.saveFailed`).
Type: error message
Context: Tells the user the favorite beverage could not be saved and invites them to retry. "Favorite beverage" = the saved (drink + volume) shortcut shown in the favorites list. Sibling of "favoriteDrinkAlreadyExists" (duplicate case). Paired with the alert title "Error" (`error()` key). Tone is polite and action-oriented.
Register: casual T-form — an error / failure message is brand-voice «ты» with a reserved tone across all T-V / honorific languages (seriousness comes from the reserved tone and impersonal phrasing, never from a formal pronoun; § Brand voice § Фамильярность). Not legally-binding text; legacy «вы» grandfathered.
Tone: reserved

t.ar: تعذّر إضافة هذا المشروب إلى المفضلة. يرجى المحاولة مرة أخرى
t.da: Kunne ikke tilføje denne favoritdrik. Prøv venligst igen
t.de: Dieses Lieblingsgetränk konnte nicht hinzugefügt werden. Bitte versuche es erneut
t.en: Couldn't add this favorite beverage. Please try again
t.es: No se pudo añadir esta bebida favorita. Inténtalo de nuevo
t.fr: Impossible d'ajouter cette boisson aux favoris. Réessaie
t.hi: यह पसंदीदा पेय जोड़ा नहीं जा सका। फिर से कोशिश करो
t.id: Tidak dapat menambahkan minuman favorit ini. Silakan coba lagi
t.it: Impossibile aggiungere questa bevanda preferita. Riprova
t.ja: お気に入りの飲み物を追加できませんでした。もう一度お試しください
t.ko: 즐겨찾기 음료를 추가할 수 없어요. 다시 시도해 주세요
t.ms: Tidak dapat menambah minuman kegemaran ini. Sila cuba lagi
t.nb: Kunne ikke legge til denne favorittdrikken. Prøv igjen
t.nl: Kan dit favoriete drankje niet toevoegen. Probeer het opnieuw
t.pl: Nie udało się dodać napoju do ulubionych. Spróbuj ponownie
t.pt_BR: Não foi possível adicionar esta bebida favorita. Tente novamente
t.ru: Не удалось добавить напиток в избранное. Попробуй снова.
t.sv: Kunde inte lägga till den här favoritdrycken. Försök igen.
t.tr: Favori içecek eklenemedi. Lütfen tekrar deneyin.
t.vi: Không thể thêm đồ uống yêu thích này. Vui lòng thử lại
t.zh_CN: 无法添加此收藏饮品。请重试

context:
Surface: Settings → App style screen — toggle / row title that switches the Home screen mascot fill style between Classic (monochrome silhouette) and Colored (full multi-color artwork). Adjacent segmented control values are characterFillStyleClassic / characterFillStyleColored.
Type: setting row title
Context: Names the setting that controls whether the mascot character on the Home screen fills with its true colors (Colored) or as a single-color silhouette (Classic) as hydration progress rises.
Constraints: Keep short for a settings row title; sentence case.

t.ja: キャラクターをカラーで塗る

context:
Surface: app-wide — volume unit suffix on main / widget / watch / Siri / notification surfaces (US / imperial units).
Type: unit abbreviation
Context: Fluid-ounce abbreviation, shown after a numeric volume value (e.g. "8 fl oz"). US / imperial volume unit, the imperial counterpart of `ml` / `l`. Note this is fluid-ounce (volume), not weight-ounce (`oz`).
Constraints: Keep short — typically ≤6 chars; use the standard abbreviation in the target language, never expand to the full phrase ("fluid ounces").

Surface: Friend endorsements (kudos) emoji picker — VoiceOver label for the water-drop emoji button. One of the three named preset emojis (wave / smile / drop); siblings: friendEndorsementWaveA11y, friendEndorsementSmileA11y.
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken description of the water-drop emoji used as an endorsement reaction. Translate the noun phrase naturally for spoken form, keeping "emoji" or its locale equivalent.
Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken noun phrase.

t.vi: Giọt nước, biểu tượng cảm xúc

context:
Surface: Friend endorsements (kudos) emoji picker — VoiceOver label for the "more emoji" / "open full emoji picker" button shown after the preset emojis.
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken description for the button that lets the user pick a custom emoji (not in the preset set) to send as an endorsement reaction. Imperative-feeling phrase suitable for spoken form.
Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken action description.

t.vi: Chọn biểu tượng cảm xúc khác

context:
Surface: Main screen "Received endorsements" (kudos) cell and friends item cells — VoiceOver accessibility hint announced when the user can tap to open a friend's profile from a received-endorsement entry.
Type: accessibility hint
Context: Spoken hint for the tap-to-open-profile action. Inserts the friend's display name as a possessive ("Open [%s] profile" → "Open Alex profile" / "Open Alex's profile" depending on locale grammar). Translate so the possessive reads naturally in target language.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — friend's display name (login or full name), e.g. "Alex"
Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken phrase.

Surface: Main screen "Received endorsements" cell and friends item cells — VoiceOver fragment describing a single received endorsement (one emoji reaction from one friend). Multiple fragments are joined together for the cell's full accessibility label.
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken phrase like "smile from Alex" / "wave from Maria" — names the emoji and the sender. Indexed placeholders allow re-ordering in target languages (e.g. some languages may prefer "от Алекса смайл" / sender-first phrasing).
Placeholders:
  [%1$s] — emoji accessibility label (e.g. "smile", "wave", "water drop")
  [%2$s] — friend display name (login or full name)
Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural; preserve the indexed placeholder syntax ([%1$s] / [%2$s]) — translator may swap their order.

Surface: Friends item cells — VoiceOver label announced for the received-endorsements icon group on a friend row (shown when the friend has sent the current user emoji reactions today).
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken summary identifying that the icons represent emoji reactions received from a friend (general descriptor; the specific emojis are announced separately via friendEndorsementReceivedA11y).
Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken noun phrase.

t.ja: 友達からの絵文字リアクション
t.vi: Biểu tượng cảm xúc từ bạn bè

context:
Surface: Friends item cell, friend profile statistics cell — VoiceOver label for the action button that withdraws an emoji endorsement the current user already sent to this friend today (tapping the button removes the previously-sent emoji).
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken action description like "Remove sent smile" / "Remove sent wave" — communicates that tapping un-sends the named emoji.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — the emoji's raw character/glyph (e.g. "👋", "🙂", "💧"); VoiceOver reads the emoji as its own spoken name when announcing
Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken action.

Surface: Friend profile statistics cell — VoiceOver label for the badge button that opens the emoji picker to send an endorsement, when no recipient name is available (generic variant; sibling friendEndorsementSendToA11y is used when the friend's name is known).
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken action description for the generic "send emoji endorsement" entry point. Imperative verb phrase.
Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken action.

Surface: Friends item cells — VoiceOver label for the badge button that opens the emoji picker to send an endorsement to a specific friend (name-aware variant; sibling friendEndorsementSendEmojiA11y is the generic variant without a name).
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken action description like "Send emoji to Alex". Inserts the recipient's display name.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — recipient friend's display name (login or full name), e.g. "Alex"
Constraints: Read aloud — keep short and natural as a spoken action.

t.vi: Gửi biểu tượng cảm xúc cho [%s]

context:
Surface: Friend endorsements emoji picker — accessibility label for a non-preset (custom / freshly typed) emoji button.
Type: accessibility label
Context: Read aloud by VoiceOver when the selected endorsement emoji is not one of the named presets (wave / smile / drop); names the emoji character itself instead of a preset label.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — the actual emoji character (e.g. "🎉")
Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Friend endorsements emoji picker — accessibility label for the "smile" preset emoji (😊).
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken by VoiceOver on the smile emoji button in the row of preset endorsement emojis sent to friends. Names the emoji so blind users can pick a specific reaction.
Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

t.vi: Mặt cười, biểu tượng cảm xúc

context:
Surface: Friend endorsements emoji picker — accessibility label for the "wave" preset emoji (👋).
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken by VoiceOver on the wave emoji button in the row of preset endorsement emojis sent to friends. Names the emoji so blind users can pick a specific reaction.
Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

t.vi: Bàn tay vẫy, biểu tượng cảm xúc

context:
Surface: Main screen "Received endorsements" (kudos) cell, accessibility label summarising today's emoji reactions from friends.
Type: accessibility label
Context: Spoken on the kudos cell so VoiceOver users hear the joined summary of emojis received today.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — comma-joined summary of received emojis and counts assembled in code (e.g. "smile, wave, 2 more kudos")
Constraints: Read aloud — must be natural spoken phrase, not a UI fragment.

Surface: Main screen "Received endorsements" (kudos) cell, visible title.
Type: section header
Context: Visible heading on the kudos pill / cell shown on the main screen above today's received emoji reactions from friends.
Constraints: Keep short (fits the kudos pill on one line).

Surface: app-wide — abbreviated unit suffix appended after sugar / caffeine numeric values (main screen sugar / caffeine cards, statistics charts, beverage details, drink creator preview, etc.).
Type: unit suffix
Context: Abbreviation for "grams" (mass unit) used as a suffix after a number (e.g. "12 g"). Use the locale-standard short form for grams in the target language (e.g. "г" in ru, "g" in de/fr, "gr" in some locales). Distinct from "ml" (volume).
Constraints: Keep ultra-short (≤3 chars); no trailing period.

en: Couldn't link to the H2OPal app
context:
Surface: Settings → Integrations → H2OPal setup screen — alert title shown when authenticating with the H2OPal third-party integration fails.
Type: alert title
Context: Headline above the underlying H2OPal error message (the body comes from `error.localizedDescription` of the H2OPal SDK, not localized by us). "H2OPal" is a third-party product name and "link" means connecting the user's account / smart-bottle to the app — keep "H2OPal" untranslated.
Constraints: Keep short to fit an alert title.

t.da: Kunne ikke oprette forbindelse til H2OPal
t.en: Couldn't link to the H2OPal app
t.es: No se pudo conectar con H2OPal
t.fr: Impossible de se connecter à H2OPal
t.hi: H2OPal ऐप से कनेक्ट नहीं हो सका
t.id: Tidak dapat menghubungkan ke aplikasi H2OPal
t.ja: H2OPalアプリと接続できませんでした
t.pl: Nie udało się połączyć z aplikacją H2OPal
t.pt_BR: Não foi possível conectar ao app H2OPal
t.vi: Không thể kết nối với H2OPal

context:
Surface: Main screen — section header action button on the caffeine / alcohol / sugar tracking cards (MainViewModel section headers).
Type: button label
Context: Short action label on the caffeine / alcohol / sugar section headers — tapping it opens the dialog to edit the daily limit for that substance. Noun meaning "daily limit / cap".
Constraints: Keep very short (≤8 chars) — fits a compact section header action.

Surface: Settings → Notifications screen — "Live Activity" selector row title and the pushed selector screen title (`NotificationsPopupIntervalsViewController`, liveActivityRow).
Type: settings row title / screen title
Context: Refers to the iOS Live Activity feature (Dynamic Island / Lock Screen live progress). "Live Activity" is the Apple platform term — preserve as Apple uses it in the target language (follow Apple's official localization).

t.ja: ライブアクティビティ

context:
Surface: Hydration Live Activity — main title on the Live Activity card (Dynamic Island expanded view, Lock Screen) (HydrationLiveActivity, HydrationSnippetView).
Type: notification title / Live Activity title
Context: Header naming what the Live Activity tracks — today's hydration progress. Short noun phrase.
Constraints: Keep very short — Live Activity has limited space.
Register: casual T-form — app confirmation / notification is casual companion voice (default for non-formal surfaces) across all T-V / honorific languages.

Surface: Hydration Live Activity — success title shown when the user reaches the daily goal (HydrationLiveActivity). Sibling: "liveActivityDrinkAddedTitle" (non-goal-reached state).
Type: notification title / Live Activity title
Context: Celebratory title that the user has reached the daily water goal. Exclamation is intentional (celebratory tone).
Constraints: Keep very short — Live Activity has limited space.
Register: casual T-form — app confirmation / notification is casual companion voice (default for non-formal surfaces) across all T-V / honorific languages.
Tone: celebratory.

Surface: Hydration Live Activity — short success title shown right after a drink is logged from the Live Activity (non-goal-reached state).
Type: notification title
Context: Confirmation that a drink was successfully added. Paired with "liveActivityCelebrationTitle" for the goal-reached state.
Constraints: Keep very short — Live Activity has limited space.
Register: casual T-form — push reminder titles use casual partner voice (default for non-formal surfaces).

t.ja: 飲み物を追加しました

context:
Surface: Settings → "Sound on add drink" screen — navigation title.
Type: screen title
Context: Title of the screen where the user picks which sound (or silence / vibration) plays each time they add a drink.

t.nb: Lyd når du legger til en drikk
t.nl: Geluid bij toevoegen van een drankje
t.vi: Âm thanh khi thêm đồ uống

en: A sound plays when you add a drink, unless your phone is in Silent Mode
context:
Surface: Settings → "Sound on add drink" screen — informational footer text shown when the device output volume is not muted.
Type: paragraph (informational)
Context: Explains the iOS Silent Mode behavior: add-drink sound respects the ringer/Silent Mode switch and won't play in Silent Mode.

t.ar: يصدر صوت عند إضافة مشروب، ما لم يكن هاتفك في الوضع الصامت
t.da: Der afspilles en lyd, når du tilføjer en drik, medmindre din telefon er på lydløs
t.de: Beim Hinzufügen eines Getränks wird ein Ton abgespielt, sofern dein Telefon nicht im Lautlosmodus ist
t.en: A sound plays when you add a drink, unless your phone is in Silent Mode
t.es: Se reproduce un sonido al añadir una bebida, salvo cuando el teléfono está en modo silencioso
t.fr: Un son est émis quand tu ajoutes une boisson, sauf si ton téléphone est en mode silencieux
t.hi: पेय जोड़ने पर साउंड बजता है, बशर्ते फ़ोन साइलेंट मोड में न हो
t.id: Suara akan berbunyi saat kamu menambah minuman, kecuali ponselmu dalam Mode Senyap
t.it: Sentirai un suono quando aggiungi una bevanda, a meno che il telefono non sia in modalità silenziosa
t.ja: サイレントモードでない場合、飲み物を追加すると音が鳴ります
t.ko: 휴대폰이 무음 모드가 아니면 음료 추가 소리가 재생돼요
t.ms: Bunyi akan dimainkan apabila awak tambah minuman, kecuali telefon dalam Mod Senyap
t.nb: Det spilles av en lyd når du legger til en drikk, med mindre telefonen din er i lydløs modus
t.nl: Er klinkt een geluid wanneer je een drankje toevoegt, tenzij je telefoon in stille modus staat
t.pl: Dźwięk jest odtwarzany przy dodawaniu napoju, jeśli telefon nie jest w trybie cichym
t.pt_BR: Um som toca ao adicionar uma bebida, a menos que seu telefone esteja no modo silencioso
t.ru: Звук добавления напитка воспроизводится, только когда телефон не в беззвучном режиме
t.sv: Ett ljud spelas upp när du lägger till en dryck, om telefonen inte är i tyst läge
t.tr: Telefon sessiz modda değilken içecek eklendiğinde bir ses çalar
t.vi: Âm thanh sẽ phát khi bạn thêm đồ uống, trừ khi điện thoại đang ở chế độ im lặng
t.zh_CN: 添加饮品时会播放提示音，手机静音时除外

en: Your phone is muted, so you won't hear the add-drink sound
context:
Surface: Settings → "Sound on add drink" screen — informational footer text shown when the device output volume is currently 0 (muted).
Type: paragraph (informational)
Context: Tells the user that because the phone is muted, the add-drink sound they pick will not actually be audible until they raise the volume.

t.ar: هاتفك في الوضع الصامت، لن تسمع صوت إضافة المشروب
t.da: Din telefon er på lydløs, så du vil ikke høre lyden ved tilføjelse af drik
t.de: Dein Telefon ist stummgeschaltet, daher hörst du den Ton beim Hinzufügen nicht
t.en: Your phone is muted, so you won't hear the add-drink sound
t.es: El teléfono está silenciado, por lo que no oirás el sonido al añadir una bebida
t.fr: Ton téléphone est en mode silencieux, tu n'entendras donc pas le son d'ajout de boisson
t.hi: तुम्हारा फ़ोन म्यूट है, इसलिए पेय जोड़ने की आवाज़ नहीं सुनाई देगी
t.id: Ponselmu dibisukan, jadi kamu tidak akan mendengar suara saat menambah minuman
t.it: Il telefono è in modalità silenziosa, quindi non sentirai il suono quando aggiungi una bevanda
t.ja: デバイスがミュートになっているため、飲み物追加時の音は聞こえません
t.ko: 휴대폰이 무음 모드라서 음료 추가 소리가 들리지 않아요
t.ms: Telefon awak dibisukan, jadi awak tidak akan mendengar bunyi tambah minuman
t.nb: Telefonen er dempet, så du hører ikke lyden når du legger til en drikk
t.nl: Je telefoon is gedempt, dus je hoort het geluid bij het toevoegen niet
t.pl: Telefon jest wyciszony, więc nie usłyszysz dźwięku dodawania napoju
t.pt_BR: Seu telefone está sem som, então você não vai ouvir o som ao adicionar uma bebida
t.ru: Звук на телефоне выключен, поэтому сигнал добавления напитка слышно не будет
t.sv: Telefonen är tystad, så du hör inte ljudet när du lägger till en dryck
t.tr: Telefonun sesi kapalı, bu yüzden içecek ekleme sesini duyamayacaksın
t.vi: Điện thoại đang tắt tiếng, bạn sẽ không nghe được âm thanh khi thêm đồ uống
t.zh_CN: 手机当前静音，你将听不到添加饮品的提示音

context:
Surface: Settings → Notifications → "Notification texts" — navigation title of the NotificationTextsViewController screen, and label of the row in the Notifications screen that opens it.
Type: screen title / settings row title
Context: Title of the screen where the user manages their own reminder notification text bodies — the list of message bodies the app may use when sending scheduled reminder pushes. Part of a new "user-defined notification texts" feature.
Constraints: Keep short to fit a navigation bar title / settings row.

Surface: Settings → Notifications interval popup (`NotificationsPopupIntervalsViewController`) — title of the toggle row that controls whether scheduled reminder pushes include the user's daily progress (drunk so far / goal).
Type: settings row title
Context: Label for a switch that, when enabled, makes the app append the user's current daily progress to scheduled reminder notification bodies. "Show … in notifications" framing.
Constraints: Keep short to fit a settings row title.

t.fr: Afficher la progression dans les notifications
t.vi: Hiển thị tiến trình trong thông báo

context:
Surface: Settings → Notifications → Notification texts screen (`NotificationTextsViewController`) — primary "add" button label (toolbar button and table-empty-state CTA) and the title of the "Add text" entry alert.
Type: button label / alert title
Context: Imperative verb phrase opening the editor for a new custom reminder notification text. "Text" here = the message body of a reminder push. Also used as the button's accessibility label.
Constraints: Keep short to fit a button label / alert title; no trailing period.

Surface: Settings → Notifications → Notification texts → "Add text" alert — text field placeholder shown while the user types a new custom reminder notification text.
Type: placeholder
Context: Hint shown inside the empty text field when the user is creating a new custom notification text body that will appear in scheduled reminder pushes.
Constraints: Keep short to fit an alert text field.

t.fr: Entre le texte de la notification

context:
Surface: Settings → Notifications → Notification texts — confirmation alert title shown after the user taps the delete (swipe / destructive) action on one of their custom notification texts.
Type: error message (confirmation alert title)
Context: Asks the user to confirm permanent deletion of a single custom notification text from their list. Paired with the "Delete" / "Cancel" alert buttons.

t.ja: この通知テキストを削除しますか？
t.tr: Bu bildirim metnini silmek istiyor musun?
t.vi: Xóa văn bản thông báo này?

en: Add another text before deleting this one
context:
Surface: Settings → Notifications → Notification texts — alert body shown when the user tries to delete the last remaining notification text from their list (NotificationTextsViewController).
Type: error message
Context: Prevents the user from ending up with zero notification texts. Asks them to add another text first before deleting the last one. Polite, action-oriented instruction (not blaming the user).
Constraints: Keep one sentence with a trailing period.

t.ar: أضف نصًا آخر قبل حذف هذا النص
t.da: Tilføj en anden tekst, før du sletter denne.
t.de: Füge zuerst einen anderen Text hinzu, bevor du diesen löschst
t.en: Add another text before deleting this one
t.es: Añade otro texto antes de eliminar este.
t.fr: Ajoute un autre texte avant de supprimer celui-ci.
t.hi: इसे हटाने से पहले दूसरा टेक्स्ट जोड़ो
t.id: Tambahkan teks lain sebelum menghapus yang ini
t.it: Aggiungi un altro testo prima di eliminare questo.
t.ja: 削除する前に別のテキストを追加してください。
t.ko: 이것을 삭제하기 전에 다른 텍스트를 추가해 주세요
t.ms: Tambah teks lain dahulu sebelum memadam yang ini.
t.nb: Legg til en annen tekst før du sletter denne
t.nl: Voeg eerst een andere tekst toe voordat je deze verwijdert
t.pl: Dodaj inny tekst, zanim usuniesz ten.
t.pt_BR: Adicione outro texto antes de excluir este
t.ru: Сначала добавь другой текст, прежде чем удалять этот.
t.sv: Lägg till en annan text innan du tar bort den här.

context:
Surface: Share screen "Save image" mode — hydration share-card content; stat label under the drinks-count value.
Type: section header (stat label)
Context: Caption under a numeric stat showing how many drinks the user logged today. Rendered uppercased at runtime; provide the natural-case source word and let display logic uppercase it.
Constraints: Keep very short — single word; no trailing period.

Surface: Share screen "Save image" mode — hydration share-card content; stat label under the streak-days value.
Type: section header (stat label)
Context: Caption under a numeric stat showing the user's current consecutive-day streak of meeting their hydration goal. Rendered uppercased at runtime; provide the natural-case source word and let display logic uppercase it.
Constraints: Keep very short — single word; no trailing period.

Surface: Share screen "Save image" mode — hydration share-card content; stat label under the percentage-vs-average value (e.g. "+12%").
Type: section header (stat label)
Context: Short caption under a numeric "+/-N%" stat that compares today's hydration progress against the user's average. Abbreviation "vs avg" stands for "versus average" — use a short locale-appropriate equivalent (e.g. "vs. сред.", "vs Ø", "ср.") rather than expanding into a full phrase. Rendered uppercased at runtime.
Constraints: Keep very short (≤8 chars / 1-2 tokens); no trailing period.

Surface: Share screen "Save image" mode — small uppercased eyebrow caption above the hero title.
Type: section header (eyebrow caption)
Context: Short top eyebrow line shown above the hero title on the share screen when sharing the daily hydration result. Rendered uppercased at runtime; provide the natural-case source phrase.
Constraints: Keep short (1-3 words / ≤20 chars); no trailing period.

Surface: Share screen "Save image" mode — primary hero title (large) inviting the user to share today's hydration result image.
Type: screen title
Context: Large hero title on the share screen when the user is about to save / share a hydration result image. "Hydration win" frames hitting today's goal as a small victory — keep the warm, encouraging framing in target language.
Constraints: Keep short (≤30 chars); no trailing period.
Tone: encouraging.

t.vi: Chia sẻ thành quả hôm nay

context:
Surface: Generated share image (PNG / screenshot saved to camera roll) — uppercased eyebrow caption rendered above the multi-line title on the image itself.
Type: section header (eyebrow caption on share image)
Context: Small uppercased line on the share image above the "Hydrated. / Energized. / Done." headline; appends a formatted date (e.g. "MY DAY · MAY 15"). The "·" is a middot separator — keep as-is. Rendered uppercased at runtime.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — locale-formatted date string (e.g. "MAY 15" / "15 МАЯ"), already prepared by code
Constraints: Keep short (1-3 words plus the date); no trailing period. and the "·" separator.

Surface: Generated share image — small tag line rendered under the hero title, naming the app brand the user tracked with.
Type: paragraph (brand tag on share image)
Context: Brand attribution line on the share image — "tracked with MyWater · mywaterapp.me". The brand string already contains the website, so the placeholder carries both the app name and URL.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — brand string with site, assembled in code (e.g. "MyWater · mywaterapp.me")
Constraints: Keep short; no trailing period.

Surface: Generated share image — second / closing line of the two-line hero headline ("Stay hydrated / with My Water"); rendered in the primary text color with the brand name [%s] highlighted in the brand accent color via substring match in code.
Type: screen title (one line of a multi-line headline with embedded brand)
Context: Closing line of a two-line brand-promotional hero on the generated share image. The full two-line composition reads "Stay hydrated / with <brand>". This line is the connector word ("with" / "с" / "avec" / "con" / …) plus the brand attribution placeholder.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — app brand name (e.g. "My Water"), assembled in code at render time. Do NOT translate / transliterate the brand; it stays as Latin script across all locales (matches the brand mark elsewhere in the image). The connector word before the placeholder IS translated.
Constraints: Keep terse (one connector word + the placeholder); the placeholder must remain at the end of the line so the brand reads as the visual punctuation; no trailing period.
Tone: friendly, supportive, brand-promotional.

Surface: Generated share image — first line of the two-line hero headline ("Stay hydrated / with My Water"); rendered in the primary text color.
Type: screen title (one line of a multi-line headline)
Context: First / opening line of a two-line brand-promotional hero on the generated share image. The full two-line composition reads "Stay hydrated / with <brand>" where <brand> is rendered on the second line in the brand accent color (assembled in code, not localized; the brand stays as a Latin-script English mark across all locales). This is a friendly promo headline that works across all share triggers (daily progress, app share, award, tip, invite).
Constraints: Keep short (fits hero); no trailing period (matches the new no-period style).
Tone: friendly, supportive, brand-promotional (not celebratory).

context:
Surface: Siri / Shortcuts — "Show current hydration" AppIntent dialog response when the daily goal is not yet reached.
Type: AppIntent dialog
Context: Spoken and on-screen summary Siri delivers reporting the user's current hydration progress vs daily goal (e.g. "You've had 800 ml of 2000 ml today (40%).").
Placeholders:
  [%1$s] — current amount drunk today with unit (e.g. "800 ml", "27 fl oz")
  [%2$s] — daily goal amount with unit (e.g. "2000 ml", "68 fl oz")
  [%3$s] — percent string of goal reached (e.g. "40%")
Constraints: Read aloud — must be a natural spoken phrase; placeholder order may change in the target language.

context:
Surface: Spotlight "Open drink" AppIntent — title shown in Shortcuts / Spotlight editors.
Type: AppIntent title
Context: Imperative verb phrase. Title of the OpenDrinkIntent that opens a beverage entry inside the app.
Constraints: Keep short.

Surface: Main screen alcohol card subtitle, OneDayStatistic alcohol summary value, accessibility values for alcohol charts.
Type: unit label
Context: Full label for the US "standard drink" unit used to quantify alcohol consumption (1 standard drink ≈ 14 g pure alcohol in the US system). Appears next to a number and is also concatenated into accessibility readouts ("3 Standard drinks (US) of 4 Standard drinks (US)").
Constraints: "(US)" parenthetical disambiguates from other countries' standard-drink definitions; keep the qualifier.

t.ja: スタンダードドリンク (US)

context:
Surface: BeverageDetailStatistic and OneDayStatistic — compact unit suffix next to a standard-drinks number in chart cells / value rows.
Type: unit label (abbreviation)
Context: Short abbreviation of "standardDrinksUS" used in compact UI where the full label doesn't fit (e.g. "1.5 std"). Refers to the US standard-drink unit for alcohol.
Constraints: Keep very short (≤4 chars) — use the locale-conventional abbreviation.

Surface: Create / edit drink screen — title of the "Sugar" field (`CreateDrinkViewController.sugarView.title`) and the explainer popup title (`showSugarAlert`); App Style → Sugar tracking section title (`AppStyleViewModel`, `AppStyleViewController`); also used as the accessibility prefix for sugar values (`DrinksParametersViewModel`).
Type: field label / section title / popup title
Context: Single-word noun naming the sugar-tracking feature / field. Refers to the per-beverage free-sugar content the user can enter on a drink.
Constraints: Keep short to fit a field label / section title.

en: Track the amount of sugar in your drink. Sugar in grams per [%s]
context:
Surface: Create / edit drink screen — explainer popup body shown when the user taps the info button next to the sugar field (`CreateDrinkViewController.showSugarAlert`). Paired title key "sugar".
Type: paragraph (informational)
Context: Two short paragraphs separated by a blank line. First tells the user that this field captures the amount of sugar in the drink (no distinction between added and naturally occurring sugar). Second explains the unit convention — sugar is entered in grams per the given reference volume (e.g. "per 100 ml"). Mirrors the sibling `caffeineDescription` pattern.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — reference volume with units, currently assembled at runtime as localized "100 ml".

context:
Surface: Create / edit drink screen — value displayed in the sugar field (`CreateDrinkViewController.set(freeSugarGramsPer100Ml:)`) and read aloud as the accessibility value for sugar in `DrinksParametersViewModel`.
Type: unit-formatted value
Context: Renders the per-beverage free-sugar value as grams per 100 ml of the beverage (the reference volume the app uses for sugar concentration). Compact form: "<value> g/100 ml".
Placeholders:
  [%s] — sugar amount as a formatted number with 1 decimal (e.g. "5.4", "0.0"); produced by `WaterNumberFormatter`.
Constraints: Keep the "g/100 ml" units in the form your locale uses for sugar concentration on nutrition labels; the "/" separator is a per-volume marker, not a fraction. Keep compact — fits next to the sugar field.

Surface: Beverage detail statistics — highlight card title for the "Average" stat (BeverageDetailStatisticViewController).
Type: section header / stat card title
Context: Title of the highlight card showing the user's average per-day amount of this beverage for the selected period. Single-word noun.
Constraints: Keep very short (≤ ~12 chars) — fits a stat card title.

Surface: Beverage detail statistics — highlight card title for the "Best day" stat (BeverageDetailStatisticViewController).
Type: section header / stat card title
Context: Title of the highlight card showing the day with the highest logged amount of this beverage during the selected period.
Constraints: Keep very short (≤ ~12 chars) — fits a stat card title.

Surface: Beverage detail statistics — chart section title and chart accessibility label (BeverageDetailStatisticViewController).
Type: section header / accessibility label
Context: Title of the per-beverage chart card on the Beverage detail statistics screen. Used both as the visible section header and as the chart view's `accessibilityLabel` for VoiceOver.
Constraints: Keep short (≤ ~25 chars) — fits a chart card header.

Surface: Beverage detail statistics — VoiceOver accessibility label for the chart when comparison mode is enabled (BeverageDetailStatisticViewController).
Type: accessibility label
Context: VoiceOver-only label that announces the chart together with the comparison period (e.g. "Beverage chart, comparison vs Last week"). Placeholder order must be flexible — translator may reorder `[%1$s]` and `[%2$s]` for natural language flow.
Placeholders:
  [%1$s] — the chart title (currently localized as "Beverage chart" from key `statisticsBeverageChart`)
  [%2$s] — the comparison period label (e.g. "Last week", "Last month")
Constraints: VoiceOver only — no character limit, but keep it one natural-sounding sentence. Use the indexed placeholders so target languages can reorder.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → chart header, label for the comparison-period column (paired with `statisticsBeverageCurrentPeriod`).
Type: section header / label
Context: Short header above the previous-period total in the two-column chart header (e.g. "vs last" / "This" pair). Followed visually by the period name ("week", "month") rendered separately. Sentence-style casing.
Constraints: Keep very short (1-2 words) — sits next to a number in a narrow header column.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → chart header, label for the current-period column (paired with `statisticsBeverageComparisonPeriod`).
Type: section header / label
Context: Short header above the current-period total in the two-column chart header (e.g. "This" / "vs last" pair). Followed visually by the period name ("week", "month") rendered separately. Sentence-style casing.
Constraints: Keep very short (1 word if possible) — sits next to a number in a narrow header column.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → Highlights grid, tile title for the entries-count metric.
Type: section header / metric label
Context: Title above the number of individual drink entries logged in the selected period (e.g. "Entries" with a count below). Noun.
Constraints: Keep short (1-2 words) to fit a compact highlights tile.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "Highlights & insights" section header.
Type: section header
Context: Header above the highlights grid (best day, average, entries count, water balance) and the tip cards. Plain noun phrase, sentence case.
Constraints: Keep concise — single section header line.

t.ja: ハイライトとインサイト
t.vi: Điểm nổi bật và phân tích

context:
Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → percentage-of-total label inside the chart / breakdown row.
Type: paragraph (inline metric)
Context: Shows a beverage's volume alongside its share of the period's total intake (e.g. "350 ml: 42% of total"). Placeholder order may be reordered by the translator if natural language flow requires it.
Placeholders:
  [%1$s] — formatted volume with unit (e.g. "350 ml", "1.2 l", "12 fl oz")
  [%2$i] — integer percentage (e.g. 42)
  [%] — universal literal-percent token rendered right after the number as a "%" sign
Constraints: Keep [%] adjacent to [%2$i] so they render as a single "42%" token.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → previous-period label when the selected period is "week".
Type: label
Context: Used as the comparison-period name (e.g. shown next to the previous-week total, or interpolated into tip cards). Sentence case noun phrase.
Constraints: Keep short (1-2 words).

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" time-of-day chart, bucket label.
Type: label (chart bucket / axis label)
Context: Name of the daytime bucket (roughly midday hours). Sibling of `statisticsBeverageTimeMorning` / `statisticsBeverageTimeEvening` / `statisticsBeverageTimeNight`. Also lowercased and reused inside tip cards (`statisticsBeverageTipLowPeriod` / `…Reminder`), so the noun must read naturally lowercased mid-sentence too.
Constraints: Single word; keep short to fit a narrow chart bucket label.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" time-of-day chart, bucket label.
Type: label (chart bucket / axis label)
Context: Name of the evening bucket. Sibling of `statisticsBeverageTimeMorning` / `statisticsBeverageTimeDay` / `statisticsBeverageTimeNight`. Also lowercased and reused inside tip cards (`statisticsBeverageTipLowPeriod` / `…Reminder`), so the noun must read naturally lowercased mid-sentence too.
Constraints: Single word; keep short to fit a narrow chart bucket label.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" time-of-day chart, bucket label.
Type: label (chart bucket / axis label)
Context: Name of the morning bucket. Sibling of `statisticsBeverageTimeDay` / `statisticsBeverageTimeEvening` / `statisticsBeverageTimeNight`. Also lowercased and reused inside tip cards (`statisticsBeverageTipLowPeriod` / `…Reminder`), so the noun must read naturally lowercased mid-sentence too.
Constraints: Single word; keep short to fit a narrow chart bucket label.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" time-of-day chart, bucket label.
Type: label (chart bucket / axis label)
Context: Name of the night bucket. Sibling of `statisticsBeverageTimeMorning` / `statisticsBeverageTimeDay` / `statisticsBeverageTimeEvening`. Lowercased version may also appear in tips, though the night bucket is currently excluded from the low-period reminder tip.
Constraints: Single word; keep short to fit a narrow chart bucket label.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" section subtitle, just below `statisticsBeverageTimeOfDayTitle`.
Type: subtitle / paragraph
Context: Explains that the time-of-day chart shows each bucket's share of the day's intake as a percentage. The value intentionally starts with a literal "%" sign — keep that "%" character as-is, do not convert it to a format specifier.
Constraints: Keep short (single line under the section title). Preserve the leading "%" literal.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → "When you drink" section title (above the time-of-day breakdown chart).
Type: section header
Context: Header for the chart that breaks the user's drinking down by time-of-day bucket (morning / day / evening / night).
Constraints: Keep concise — single section header line.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → first tip card, "behind" variant — the emphasized volume fragment that gets embedded into `statisticsBeverageTipDown`.
Type: tip (fragment)
Context: Short phrase meaning "<amount> behind the previous period's pace", used as the visually emphasized substring inside the down-trend tip. Example assembled sentence: "You're 200 ml behind last week's pace.".
Placeholders:
  [%s] — formatted volume with unit (e.g. "200 ml", "0.2 l", "7 fl oz")
Constraints: Keep short — this fragment must read naturally when interpolated into `statisticsBeverageTipDown`.
Register: casual T-form — tips are friendly health-conscious companion voice, not medical lecture (default for non-formal surfaces, see § Brand voice § Pronouns).
Tone: encouraging — observational, not blaming.

Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → first tip card, shown when the user drank less than in the previous comparable period.
Type: tip
Context: Negative-trend tip; [%1$s] is the already-assembled "behind" fragment (e.g. "200 ml behind") and [%2$s] is the localized period noun, lowercased (e.g. "week", "month").
Placeholders:
  [%1$s] — emphasized amount fragment from statisticsBeverageTipBehind (e.g. "200 ml behind")
  [%2$s] — period noun, lowercased (e.g. "week", "month", "year", "day")
Constraints: Indexed placeholders — reorder [%1$s] / [%2$s] freely for natural word order.
Register: casual T-form — tips are friendly health-conscious companion voice, not medical lecture (default for non-formal surfaces, see § Brand voice § Pronouns).
Tone: encouraging — observational, supportive (not blaming the user for falling behind).

t.da: Du er [%1$s] i forhold til sidste [%2$s]
t.fr: Tu as bu [%1$s] que la période précédente ([%2$s])
t.it: [%1$s] rispetto al ritmo del periodo precedente ([%2$s])
t.nl: Je loopt [%1$s] op het tempo van afgelopen [%2$s]

en: You don't drink much in the [%s]
context:
Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → second tip card, shown when the user drinks little during one time-of-day bucket and reminders are already enabled.
Type: tip
Context: Observational tip pointing out a low-intake time-of-day bucket. The placeholder is a lowercased time-of-day name (e.g. "morning", "day", "evening") taken from the `statisticsBeverageTime*` strings.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — lowercased time-of-day bucket name (e.g. "morning", "day", "evening")
Constraints: Preserve the trailing period. The placeholder will be lowercased at runtime — make sure the sentence still reads naturally with a lowercased noun there.
Register: casual T-form — tips are friendly health-conscious companion voice, not medical lecture (default for non-formal surfaces, see § Brand voice § Pronouns).
Tone: encouraging — gentle observation, not nagging.

t.da: Du drikker ikke meget på dette tidspunkt: [%s].
t.de: Tageszeit, in der du wenig trinkst: [%s].
t.en: You don't drink much in the [%s]
t.es: Periodo en el que bebes poco: [%s].
t.hi: [%s] में तुम कम पानी पीते हो

en: You barely drink in the [%s]. Want a reminder?
context:
Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → second tip card, shown when the user drinks little during one time-of-day bucket and reminders are NOT enabled (offers to turn on a reminder).
Type: tip
Context: Same observational tip as `statisticsBeverageTipLowPeriod` but with a soft follow-up question offering a reminder. Placeholder is a lowercased time-of-day name.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — lowercased time-of-day bucket name (e.g. "morning", "day", "evening")
Constraints: Keep both sentences. The placeholder will be lowercased at runtime — make sure the sentence reads naturally with a lowercased noun.
Register: casual T-form — tips are friendly health-conscious companion voice, not medical lecture (default for non-formal surfaces, see § Brand voice § Pronouns).
Tone: encouraging — gentle observation followed by a soft offer, not pushy.

context:
Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → first tip card, "up" variant — the emphasized volume fragment that gets embedded into `statisticsBeverageTipUp`.
Type: tip (fragment)
Context: Short phrase meaning "<amount> more than the previous period", used as the visually emphasized substring inside the up-trend tip. Example assembled sentence: "You drank 200 ml more than last week. Keep it up.".
Placeholders:
  [%s] — formatted volume with unit (e.g. "200 ml", "0.2 l", "7 fl oz")
Constraints: Keep short — must read naturally when interpolated into `statisticsBeverageTipUp`.
Register: casual T-form — tips are friendly health-conscious companion voice, not medical lecture (default for non-formal surfaces, see § Brand voice § Pronouns).
Tone: encouraging.

en: You drank [%1$s] than last [%2$s]. Keep it up
context:
Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → first tip card, shown when the user drank more than in the previous comparable period.
Type: tip
Context: Positive-trend tip; [%1$s] is the already-assembled "more" fragment (e.g. "200 ml more") and [%2$s] is the localized period noun, lowercased (e.g. "week", "month"). Closing "Keep it up" is friendly encouragement.
Placeholders:
  [%1$s] — emphasized amount fragment from statisticsBeverageTipMore (e.g. "200 ml more")
  [%2$s] — period noun, lowercased (e.g. "week", "month", "year", "day")
Constraints: Indexed placeholders — reorder [%1$s] / [%2$s] freely for natural word order. Keep the period between the two sentences (none after "Keep it up").
Register: casual T-form — tips are friendly health-conscious companion voice, not medical lecture (default for non-formal surfaces, see § Brand voice § Pronouns).
Tone: encouraging — celebratory but not loud (no "!").

t.da: Du drak [%1$s] end sidste [%2$s]. Bliv ved

context:
Surface: Statistics → Beverage detail → header eyebrow above the period total.
Type: section header (eyebrow / overline)
Context: Small "Total" label rendered above the total volume number for the selected period. Noun.
Constraints: Keep very short (1 word).

Surface: Onboarding paywall — Statistics preview card, third sample row date label (paired with "Today" and "Yesterday" rows above it).
Type: sample date label (mock data)
Context: Hardcoded sample calendar date shown inside a fake statistics card on the paywall to illustrate what the in-app history will look like. Not a live date. Use the translator's locale-natural format for "21 April" (day + month, no year, short month name allowed); the card is narrow so keep it brief.
Constraints: Keep short (≤10 chars) — the sample row is narrow.

Surface: Settings → App Style — row title for the "Volume display" option and the title of the resulting picker screen.
Type: settings row title / screen title
Context: Lets the user pick how drink volumes are displayed throughout the app (e.g. raw ml/oz vs. glasses / cups). Noun phrase.
Constraints: Keep short to fit a settings row title.

Surface: Main / widget / watch hydration progress text — "drunk so far" amount renderer (`WaterAmountDisplayFormat.string(currentMl:goalMl:units:)`, `.drunk` case). Compare `.outOf` (current of goal) and `waterAmountRemaining` (`.remaining`).
Type: paragraph (progress summary)
Context: Short status sentence shown next to / under the hydration progress bar, communicating how much water the user has consumed so far today. The English source uses past-participle "drunk" followed by the amount.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — formatted volume with units (e.g. "1.5 L", "1500 ml", "12 fl oz"); composed at runtime from the user's preferred units. Reorder relative to the verb if your locale puts the verb after the amount.
Constraints: Lowercase as in source (sits as a short label, not a sentence header); keep concise to fit progress text surfaces.

Surface: Main / widget / watch hydration progress text — "remaining to goal" amount renderer (`WaterAmountDisplayFormat.string(currentMl:goalMl:units:)`, `.remaining` case). Compare `.outOf` (current of goal) and `waterAmountDrunk` (`.drunk`).
Type: paragraph (progress summary)
Context: Short status phrase shown next to / under the hydration progress bar, telling the user how much water is still needed to reach today's goal. The English source uses "<amount> left" — amount first, "left" after.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — formatted volume with units (e.g. "0.5 L", "500 ml", "5 fl oz"); composed at runtime from the user's preferred units. Reorder relative to "left" if your locale puts the qualifier before the amount.
Constraints: Lowercase as in source (short progress label, not a sentence header); keep concise to fit progress text surfaces.

Surface: Settings → App Style → Tabs section — title of the toggle row that adds / removes the Weight tracker tab; also the navigation title of the Weight tracker settings screen.
Type: settings row title / screen title
Context: Names the weight tracking feature as a toggleable section the user can show or hide. "Tracker" framing matches how the app refers to its tracking surfaces.
Constraints: Keep the visible part short to fit a settings row title.

Surface: Apple Watch app — Daily drinks list, empty-state message shown when no drinks have been logged for the day yet.
Type: empty state
Context: Tells the user that no drinks have been logged for the current day on the Watch companion. Below it sits the Add button.
Constraints: Short — Watch screens are narrow; allow wrapping to multiple lines.

context:
Surface: Apple Watch — Premium Unlock screen title, shown after the user taps a locked premium drink in the drink picker.
Type: screen title (paywall hero)
Context: Hero headline naming which specific drink the user just tried to add (e.g. "Unlock Beer"). Sits above the watchPremiumSubtitle and the "Get full version" CTA.
Placeholders:
  [%s] — drink display name (e.g. "Beer", "Coffee") — capitalization comes from the drink name itself, leave the placeholder unchanged
Constraints: Watch-narrow — keep to 1-2 short lines, no trailing period.
Register: casual T-form — a paywall hero / CTA is brand-voice marketing «ты» across all T-V / honorific languages (a warm ask, not the legally-binding subscription-terms block — that would be Register: formal; § Brand voice § Юридический carve-out). Legacy «вы» grandfathered.

Surface: Apple Watch — Premium Offers screen, price line on a subscription offer card for users who ARE eligible for the free trial.
Type: subscription price line
Context: Shown on offers that include an introductory free trial — frames the offer as "trial first, then the recurring price". Pairs with the recurring price text that is composed separately (e.g. "$29.99 / year").
Placeholders:
  [%s] — formatted recurring price after the trial ends (e.g. "$29.99 / year")
Constraints: Watch-narrow — keep concise; comma after "free" reads naturally on one line.
Register: casual T-form — a paywall hero / CTA is brand-voice marketing «ты» across all T-V / honorific languages (a warm ask, not the legally-binding subscription-terms block — that would be Register: formal; § Brand voice § Юридический carve-out). Legacy «вы» grandfathered.

Surface: Widget gallery — accessoryCircular volume variant widget title (lock screen / watch face).
Type: widget gallery title
Context: Circular widget variant centred on the consumed water volume number.
Constraints: Keep very short (<=20 chars). Widget gallery title in iOS Add Widget UI; truncates aggressively.

en: Circular lock screen widget showing today's water volume
context:
Surface: Widget gallery — accessoryCircular volume variant widget description, shown beneath the title.
Type: widget gallery description
Context: Circular Volume variant — small lock screen / watch face widget showing the consumed water volume value.
Constraints: 1 short sentence; widget gallery typically truncates at 2 lines.

t.ar: أداة دائرية لشاشة القفل تعرض حجم ماء اليوم
t.da: Cirkulær låseskærmswidget, der viser dagens vandmængde
t.en: Circular lock screen widget showing today's water volume
t.es: Widget circular de pantalla bloqueada con el volumen de agua de hoy
t.fr: Widget circulaire d'écran verrouillé affichant le volume d'eau du jour
t.hi: सर्कुलर लॉक स्क्रीन विजेट जो आज की पानी की मात्रा दिखाता है

t.it: Widget circolare della schermata di blocco con il volume d'acqua di oggi

t.ko: 오늘 마신 물의 양을 원형으로 보여주는 잠금 화면 위젯
t.ms: Widget skrin kunci bulat yang menunjukkan jumlah air hari ini
t.nb: Sirkulær låseskjermwidget som viser dagens vannvolum
t.nl: Cirkelvormige vergrendelschermwidget met de hoeveelheid water van vandaag
t.pl: Okrągły widżet ekranu blokady z dzisiejszą objętością wody
t.pt_BR: Widget circular da tela bloqueada mostrando o volume de água de hoje
t.ru: Круговой виджет на экране блокировки с объёмом воды за сегодня.
t.sv: Cirkulär widget på låsskärmen som visar dagens vattenmängd
t.tr: Bugünün su hacmini gösteren dairesel kilit ekranı widget'ı

t.zh_CN: 圆形锁屏小组件，展示今日饮水量

context:
Surface: Settings → Notifications → Notification interval popup — row title for the "custom interval" option (IntervalsPopupViewController / IntervalsPopupViewModel). Also used as the duration-picker screen title when the user picks a custom value.
Type: option title / screen title
Context: Adjective + noun. Label for the option that lets the user pick a custom reminder interval (instead of the preset choices). Tapping it opens a wheel duration picker. Distinct from "customPeriod" / "customRange" used for statistics date ranges.
Constraints: Keep short to fit a popup row / navigation bar title. No trailing period.

Surface: Onboarding paywall — hero title at the top of the subscribe screen, first (top) line.
Type: hero title — first line of a composed two-line headline
Context: Opening half of the onboarding paywall headline. Concatenated visually with "onboardingSubscribeTitleHighlightedLine" on the line below to form a single sentence "Start drinking more water / with My Water app", where the second line is rendered with a highlight underline behind it. Translate as the first half of one continuous sentence — keep the natural pause / word order so the highlighted second line reads as the emphasised tail of the same phrase. Also concatenated into the VoiceOver label for the whole title.
Constraints: 1 short line; auto-shrinks but truncates on long phrasing. Avoid trailing punctuation — the sentence continues on the next line.

t.ar: ابدأ في شرب المزيد من الماء
t.ja: もっと水を飲み始めましょう

context:
Surface: Onboarding paywall — hero title at the top of the subscribe screen, second (bottom) line rendered with a hand-drawn highlight stroke behind the text.
Type: hero title — emphasised second line of a composed two-line headline
Context: Closing half of the onboarding paywall headline; pairs with "onboardingSubscribeTitleFirstLine" above to form "Start drinking more water / with My Water app". This line is the emphasised tail of the sentence — keep the brand name "My Water" exactly as written (do not translate or transliterate the product name). Also concatenated into the VoiceOver label for the whole title.
Constraints: 1 short line; auto-shrinks but truncates on long phrasing. Must read naturally as the continuation of the first line.

Surface: iOS app bundle metadata — CFBundleName in Info.plist (short app name iOS uses internally and in some system UI).
Type: brand name
Context: The app's short name. Brand — keep "My Water" verbatim in every locale unless a market ships a localized brand; do not translate the words literally.
Constraints: Do not translate (brand name); keep very short (CFBundleName is truncated by iOS).

Surface: iOS Home Screen quick action (long-press / 3D Touch on the app icon) — shortcut title for logging weight.
Type: quick action title
Context: Title of the Home Screen quick-action shortcut that opens weight logging. Noun ("body weight"), not the verb "to weigh".
Constraints: Very short — Home Screen quick-action titles are truncated; no trailing period.

en: Our advertising partners may use calendar access to help deliver relevant ads and measure ad performance.
context:
Surface: System iOS permission prompt — purpose string shown when the app requests access to the user's Calendar (Info.plist `NSCalendarsUsageDescription`). The app itself does not use the Calendar; the key exists because an integrated advertising SDK may request the permission.
Type: permission prompt
Context: Justifies why the calendar permission may be requested — to allow advertising partners to deliver more relevant ads and measure ad performance. Shown by iOS in the standard authorization sheet on first request; user can grant or deny. Apple ATT-adjacent disclosure: be specific that the access path is via an ad SDK, not direct app feature.
Constraints: Apple guideline — describe purpose specifically and concisely; first-person plural ("our advertising partners") mirrors the surrounding ad-SDK permission strings.
Register: casual T-form — a routine permission prompt is brand-voice «ты» (reserved/reasoned tone) across all T-V / honorific languages; it is NOT a legally-binding consent contract (§ Brand voice § Юридический carve-out). Legacy «вы» here is grandfathered, not a must-fix.
Tone: reasoned and direct.

t.ar: قد يستخدم شركاؤنا الإعلانيون الوصول إلى التقويم للمساعدة في عرض إعلانات مناسبة وقياس أداء الإعلانات.
t.da: Vores annoncepartnere kan bruge kalenderadgang til at vise relevante annoncer og måle annoncernes effekt.
t.de: Unsere Werbepartner können den Kalenderzugriff nutzen, um relevante Anzeigen auszuliefern und deren Leistung zu messen.
t.en: Our advertising partners may use calendar access to help deliver relevant ads and measure ad performance.
t.es: Nuestros socios publicitarios pueden usar el acceso al calendario para mostrar anuncios relevantes y medir su rendimiento.
t.fr: Nos partenaires publicitaires peuvent utiliser l'accès au calendrier pour diffuser des publicités pertinentes et en mesurer les performances.
t.hi: हमारे विज्ञापन साझेदार प्रासंगिक विज्ञापन दिखाने और उनके प्रदर्शन को मापने के लिए कैलेंडर एक्सेस का उपयोग कर सकते हैं।
t.id: Mitra iklan kami dapat menggunakan akses kalender untuk menayangkan iklan yang relevan dan mengukur kinerjanya.
t.it: I nostri partner pubblicitari possono usare l'accesso al calendario per mostrare annunci pertinenti e misurarne le prestazioni.
t.ja: 広告パートナーが関連性の高い広告の配信と効果測定のためにカレンダーへのアクセスを使用する場合があります。
t.ko: 광고 파트너가 관련성 높은 광고 게재 및 성과 측정을 위해 캘린더 접근 권한을 사용할 수 있습니다.
t.ms: Rakan pengiklanan kami mungkin menggunakan akses kalendar untuk memaparkan iklan berkaitan dan mengukur prestasinya.
t.nb: Annonsepartnerne våre kan bruke kalendertilgang for å vise relevante annonser og måle effekten av dem.
t.nl: Onze advertentiepartners kunnen toegang tot je agenda gebruiken om relevante advertenties te tonen en de prestaties ervan te meten.
t.pl: Nasi partnerzy reklamowi mogą używać dostępu do kalendarza, aby wyświetlać trafne reklamy i mierzyć ich skuteczność.
t.pt_BR: Nossos parceiros de publicidade podem usar o acesso ao calendário para exibir anúncios relevantes e medir o desempenho deles.
t.ru: Наши рекламные партнёры могут использовать доступ к календарю для показа релевантной рекламы и оценки её эффективности.
t.sv: Våra annonspartner kan använda kalenderåtkomst för att visa relevanta annonser och mäta deras effekt.
t.tr: Reklam ortaklarımız ilgili reklamları göstermek ve performanslarını ölçmek için takvim erişimini kullanabilir.
t.vi: Đối tác quảng cáo của chúng tôi có thể dùng quyền truy cập lịch để hiển thị quảng cáo phù hợp và đo lường hiệu quả.
t.zh_CN: 我们的广告合作伙伴可能会使用日历访问权限来投放相关广告并衡量广告效果。

en: Our advertising partners may use your location to help deliver relevant ads and measure ad performance.
context:
Surface: System iOS permission prompt — purpose string shown when the app requests background ("always") access to the user's location (Info.plist `NSLocationAlwaysUsageDescription`). The app itself does not use Location; the key exists because an integrated advertising SDK may request the permission.
Type: permission prompt
Context: Justifies why background location may be requested — to allow advertising partners to deliver more relevant location-aware ads and measure ad performance. Shown by iOS in the standard authorization sheet on first request; user can grant Always / While Using / deny. Apple ATT-adjacent disclosure: be specific that the access path is via an ad SDK, not a direct app feature.
Constraints: Apple guideline — describe purpose specifically and concisely; first-person plural ("our advertising partners") mirrors the surrounding ad-SDK permission strings.
Register: casual T-form — a routine permission prompt is brand-voice «ты» (reserved/reasoned tone) across all T-V / honorific languages; it is NOT a legally-binding consent contract (§ Brand voice § Юридический carve-out). Legacy «вы» here is grandfathered, not a must-fix.
Tone: reasoned and direct.

t.ja: 広告パートナーが関連性の高い広告の配信と効果測定のために位置情報を使用する場合があります。
t.ko: 광고 파트너가 관련성 높은 광고 게재 및 성과 측정을 위해 위치 정보를 사용할 수 있습니다.
t.ms: Rakan pengiklanan kami mungkin menggunakan lokasi awak untuk memaparkan iklan berkaitan dan mengukur prestasinya.
t.nb: Annonsepartnerne våre kan bruke posisjonen din til å vise relevante annonser og måle effekten av dem.
t.nl: Onze advertentiepartners kunnen je locatie gebruiken om relevante advertenties te tonen en de prestaties ervan te meten.
t.pl: Nasi partnerzy reklamowi mogą używać twojej lokalizacji, aby wyświetlać trafne reklamy i mierzyć ich skuteczność.
t.pt_BR: Nossos parceiros de publicidade podem usar sua localização para exibir anúncios relevantes e medir o desempenho deles.
t.ru: Наши рекламные партнёры могут использовать твою геопозицию для показа релевантной рекламы и оценки её эффективности.
t.sv: Våra annonspartner kan använda din plats för att visa relevanta annonser och mäta deras effekt.
t.tr: Reklam ortaklarımız sana ilgili reklamlar göstermek ve performanslarını ölçmek için konumunu kullanabilir.
t.vi: Đối tác quảng cáo của chúng tôi có thể dùng vị trí của bạn để hiển thị quảng cáo phù hợp và đo lường hiệu quả.
t.zh_CN: 我们的广告合作伙伴可能会使用你的位置来投放相关广告并衡量广告效果。

t.zh_CN: 我们的广告合作伙伴可能会使用你的位置来投放相关广告并衡量广告效果。

en: My Water uses device motion to animate the water wave on the main screen.
context:
Surface: System iOS permission prompt — purpose string shown when the app requests access to device motion (Info.plist `NSMotionUsageDescription`).
Type: permission prompt
Context: Justifies why the app reads device motion — to drive the animated water-wave effect on the main screen. Shown by iOS in the standard authorization sheet on first request; user can grant or deny.
Constraints: Apple guideline — describe purpose specifically and concisely.
Register: casual T-form — a routine permission prompt is brand-voice «ты» (reserved/reasoned tone) across all T-V / honorific languages; it is NOT a legally-binding consent contract (§ Brand voice § Юридический carve-out). Legacy «вы» here is grandfathered, not a must-fix.
Tone: reasoned and direct.

t.ar: يستخدم My Water بيانات حركة الجهاز لتحريك موجة الماء على الشاشة الرئيسية.
t.da: Mit vand bruger enhedens bevægelse til at animere vandbølgen på hovedskærmen.
t.de: My Water nutzt die Gerätebewegung, um die Wasserwelle auf dem Hauptbildschirm zu animieren.
t.en: My Water uses device motion to animate the water wave on the main screen.
t.es: My Water usa el movimiento del dispositivo para animar la ola de agua en la pantalla principal.
t.fr: My Water utilise le mouvement de l'appareil pour animer la vague d'eau sur l'écran principal.
t.hi: My Water मुख्य स्क्रीन पर पानी की लहर को एनिमेट करने के लिए डिवाइस की गति का उपयोग करता है।
t.id: My Water menggunakan gerakan perangkat untuk menganimasikan gelombang air di layar utama.
t.it: My Water usa il movimento del dispositivo per animare l'onda d'acqua nella schermata principale.
t.ja: My Water はデバイスのモーションを使ってメイン画面の水の波をアニメーション表示します。
t.ko: My Water는 기기 모션을 사용해 메인 화면의 물결을 애니메이션으로 표시합니다.
t.ms: My Water menggunakan gerakan peranti untuk menganimasikan gelombang air pada skrin utama.
t.nb: Mitt Vann bruker enhetens bevegelse for å animere vannbølgen på hovedskjermen.
t.nl: My Water gebruikt apparaatbeweging om de watergolf op het hoofdscherm te animeren.
t.pl: My Water używa ruchu urządzenia do animowania fali wody na ekranie głównym.
t.pt_BR: O My Water usa o movimento do dispositivo para animar a onda de água na tela principal.
t.ru: Приложение использует данные о движении устройства, чтобы анимировать волну воды на главном экране.
t.sv: My Water använder enhetens rörelse för att animera vattenvågen på huvudskärmen.
t.tr: My Water, ana ekrandaki su dalgasını canlandırmak için cihaz hareketini kullanır.
t.vi: My Water dùng chuyển động của thiết bị để tạo hiệu ứng sóng nước trên màn hình chính.
t.zh_CN: My Water 使用设备运动数据在主屏幕上为水波添加动画效果。

en: We need permission to save images from My Water to your photo library.
context:
Surface: System iOS permission prompt — purpose string shown when the app requests add-only access to the Photo Library (Info.plist `NSPhotoLibraryAddUsageDescription`).
Type: permission prompt
Context: Justifies why the app needs to save to the Photo Library — to store images the user generates / shares from the app. Add-only access (saving, not browsing); distinct from NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription, which grants read access for picking an avatar. Shown by iOS on first save.
Constraints: Apple guideline — describe purpose specifically and concisely; first-person plural ("we") matches app voice.
Register: casual T-form — a routine permission prompt is brand-voice «ты» (reserved/reasoned tone) across all T-V / honorific languages; it is NOT a legally-binding consent contract (§ Brand voice § Юридический carve-out). Legacy «вы» here is grandfathered, not a must-fix.
Tone: reasoned and direct.

t.ar: نحتاج إلى إذن لحفظ الصور من My Water في مكتبة صورك.
t.da: Vi har brug for tilladelse til at gemme billeder fra Mit vand i dit fotobibliotek.
t.de: Wir brauchen die Berechtigung, Bilder aus My Water in deiner Fotomediathek zu speichern.
t.en: We need permission to save images from My Water to your photo library.
t.es: Necesitamos permiso para guardar imágenes de My Water en tu fototeca.
t.fr: Nous avons besoin de l'autorisation d'enregistrer des images de My Water dans ta photothèque.
t.hi: हमें My Water की छवियाँ तुम्हारी फ़ोटो लाइब्रेरी में सहेजने की अनुमति चाहिए।
t.id: Kami memerlukan izin untuk menyimpan gambar dari My Water ke pustaka fotomu.
t.it: Ci serve l'autorizzazione per salvare le immagini di My Water nella tua libreria foto.
t.ja: My Water の画像を写真ライブラリに保存するために許可が必要です。
t.ko: My Water의 이미지를 사진 보관함에 저장하려면 권한이 필요합니다.
t.ms: Kami memerlukan kebenaran untuk menyimpan imej daripada My Water ke pustaka foto awak.
t.nb: Vi trenger tillatelse til å lagre bilder fra Mitt Vann i fotobiblioteket ditt.
t.nl: We hebben toestemming nodig om afbeeldingen van My Water in je fotobibliotheek op te slaan.
t.pl: Potrzebujemy pozwolenia na zapisywanie obrazów z My Water w twojej bibliotece zdjęć.
t.pt_BR: Precisamos de permissão para salvar imagens do My Water na sua fototeca.
t.ru: Нам нужно разрешение, чтобы сохранять изображения из «Моя вода» в твою медиатеку.
t.sv: Vi behöver behörighet att spara bilder från My Water i ditt fotobibliotek.
t.tr: My Water'daki görselleri fotoğraf kitaplığına kaydetmek için iznine ihtiyacımız var.
t.vi: Vui lòng cho phép chúng tôi lưu hình ảnh từ My Water vào thư viện ảnh của bạn.
t.zh_CN: 我们需要权限，以将 My Water 中的图片保存到你的照片图库。

en: Our advertising partners may use Bluetooth signals to help deliver relevant ads and measure ad performance.
context:
Surface: System iOS permission prompt — purpose string shown when the app requests access to Bluetooth (Info.plist `NSBluetoothAlwaysUsageDescription`). The app itself does not use Bluetooth; the key exists because an integrated advertising SDK may request the permission for proximity / signal-based ad delivery.
Type: permission prompt
Context: Justifies why Bluetooth permission may be requested — to allow advertising partners to use Bluetooth signals (e.g. nearby-device proximity) for more relevant ads and ad performance measurement. Shown by iOS in the standard authorization sheet on first request; user can grant or deny. Apple ATT-adjacent disclosure: be specific that the access path is via an ad SDK, not a direct app feature.
Constraints: Apple guideline — describe purpose specifically and concisely; first-person plural ("our advertising partners") mirrors the surrounding ad-SDK permission strings.
Register: casual T-form — a routine permission prompt is brand-voice «ты» (reserved/reasoned tone) across all T-V / honorific languages; it is NOT a legally-binding consent contract (§ Brand voice § Юридический carve-out). Legacy «вы» here is grandfathered, not a must-fix.
Tone: reasoned and direct.

t.ar: قد يستخدم شركاؤنا الإعلانيون إشارات Bluetooth للمساعدة في عرض إعلانات مناسبة وقياس أداء الإعلانات.
t.da: Vores annoncepartnere kan bruge Bluetooth-signaler til at vise relevante annoncer og måle deres effekt.
t.de: Unsere Werbepartner können Bluetooth-Signale nutzen, um relevante Anzeigen auszuliefern und deren Leistung zu messen.
t.en: Our advertising partners may use Bluetooth signals to help deliver relevant ads and measure ad performance.
t.es: Nuestros socios publicitarios pueden usar señales Bluetooth para mostrar anuncios relevantes y medir su rendimiento.
t.fr: Nos partenaires publicitaires peuvent utiliser des signaux Bluetooth pour diffuser des publicités pertinentes et en mesurer les performances.
t.hi: हमारे विज्ञापन साझेदार प्रासंगिक विज्ञापन दिखाने और उनके प्रदर्शन को मापने के लिए ब्लूटूथ सिग्नल का उपयोग कर सकते हैं।
t.id: Mitra iklan kami dapat menggunakan sinyal Bluetooth untuk menayangkan iklan yang relevan dan mengukur kinerjanya.
t.it: I nostri partner pubblicitari possono usare i segnali Bluetooth per mostrare annunci pertinenti e misurarne le prestazioni.
t.ja: 広告パートナーが関連性の高い広告の配信と効果測定のために Bluetooth 信号を使用する場合があります。
t.ko: 광고 파트너가 관련성 높은 광고 게재 및 성과 측정을 위해 블루투스 신호를 사용할 수 있습니다.
t.ms: Rakan pengiklanan kami mungkin menggunakan isyarat Bluetooth untuk memaparkan iklan berkaitan dan mengukur prestasinya.
t.nb: Annonsepartnerne våre kan bruke Bluetooth-signaler til å vise relevante annonser og måle effekten av dem.
t.nl: Onze advertentiepartners kunnen Bluetooth-signalen gebruiken om relevante advertenties te tonen en de prestaties ervan te meten.
t.pl: Nasi partnerzy reklamowi mogą używać sygnałów Bluetooth, aby wyświetlać trafne reklamy i mierzyć ich skuteczność.
t.pt_BR: Nossos parceiros de publicidade podem usar sinais Bluetooth para exibir anúncios relevantes e medir o desempenho deles.
t.ru: Наши рекламные партнёры могут использовать сигналы Bluetooth для показа релевантной рекламы и оценки её эффективности.
t.sv: Våra annonspartner kan använda Bluetooth-signaler för att visa relevanta annonser och mäta deras effekt.
t.tr: Reklam ortaklarımız ilgili reklamları göstermek ve performanslarını ölçmek için Bluetooth sinyallerini kullanabilir.
t.vi: Đối tác quảng cáo của chúng tôi có thể dùng tín hiệu Bluetooth để hiển thị quảng cáo phù hợp và đo lường hiệu quả.
t.zh_CN: 我们的广告合作伙伴可能会使用蓝牙信号来投放相关广告并衡量广告效果。

en: Could not process this photo. Please choose another image.

Type: error message

  Meaning: “Photo” refers to the avatar/profile image.
  User action: The user should choose another image.
Constraints: No placeholders. No pluralization. Keep concise.

en: Could not save the photo. Please try again.
context:
Surface: Account editing screen / avatar flow.
Type: error message
Context: Shown after returning from the camera when the app cannot recover or read the captured temporary image.

  Meaning: The local captured photo file could not be saved, recovered, or opened by the app.
  User action: The user should try taking the photo again.

context:

Type: error message
Context: Shown when the app failed to open the camera capture UI.

  Important: Do not phrase this as a permission-only error.
  User action: The user can retry or choose another image source.
Constraints: No placeholders. No pluralization. Keep concise.

context:

Type: error message

  Important: This is not a photo-library permission denial.
  Do not translate it as “allow photo access”.
  User action: The user can retry or choose another image source.
